Title: Data Analyst (Power BI / Fabric)
Description:
En Logicalis Spain estamos buscando 2 perfiles de Data Analyst para apoyar la explotación analítica de los datos corporativos y la construcción de soluciones de reporting avanzadas.

El equipo de Data & AI de Logicalis trabaja con clientes nacionales e internacionales, llevando a cabo importantes proyectos de ingeniería de datos, inteligencia artificial y analítica avanzada, estrategia del dato, gobierno, integración y arquitectura, abarcando todo el ciclo de vida del dato.

RESPONSABILIDADES:


Diseñar y desarrollar dashboards e informes en Power BI / Fabric.
Construir y mantener modelos semánticos optimizados.
Traducir necesidades de negocio en soluciones analíticas.
Definir y documentar KPIs y métricas corporativas.
Analizar datos para identificar tendencias, oportunidades y áreas de mejora.
Garantizar la calidad y consistencia de la información presentada.
Colaborar con equipos de Data Engineering y negocio para asegurar una correcta explotación del dato.


REQUISITOS:


Experiencia de entre 2 y 5 años como Data Analyst o Business Intelligence Analyst.
Experiencia en diseño y desarrollo de dashboards corporativos.
Experiencia en modelado semántico.
Experiencia demostrable principalmente en Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, DAX y Power Query.
Valorable uso de Inteligencia Artificial aplicada al proceso analítico.


BENEFICIOS

> Incorporación inmediata a compañía líder del sector IT con un alto grado de expertise en el área de Data & Analytics dónde nos encontramos en pleno proceso de expansión.

> Estabilidad laboral a través de contrato indefinido con amplias oportunidades de desarrollo profesional y crecimiento en la compañía.

> Modalidad de trabajo 100% remoto.

> Paquete retributivo muy competitivo acorde a la valía del candidato.

> Posibilidad de acogerse a planes de retribución flexible (tarjeta restaurante, tarjeta transporte y tarjeta guardería).

> Seguro médico y GYMPASS.

> Planes de formación adaptados a cada perfil (cursos técnicos, certificaciones oficiales, formación de idiomas...).

> Portal de descuentos especiales para empleados.

> Buen ambiente de trabajo y entorno muy colaborativo.

Descubre de esta oferta y muchas más en nuestra Página de Empleo. 🚀

Title: Data Analyst, External Fulfillment EU
Description:
Description

The EU External Fulfillment Ops Excellence team is looking for a hands-on, detail oriented and highly motivated Data Analyst to help create data backed insights. The data analyst will be responsible to analyze data to support support programs, dig deep into data to determine root cause of defects, build and maintain decision support tools, and present findings to business partners. In addition the Data Analyst will also be responsible for maintaining key metrics for business reviews.

Key job responsibilities


Assist in retrieving and aggregating data from multiple sources to support decision-making processes
Help maintain and review KPIs on a daily, weekly, and monthly basis
Support the EU External Fulfillment Network with basic data requests
Contribute to data analysis that informs recommendations for EU EF Leadership
Participate in data mining and basic problem-solving tasks


A day in the life

This position is office-based.

Every day is different in this role. The work you’ll conduct is both proactive and reactive, requiring you to analyse the capacity per truck for the transportation network and work quickly to resolve any operational issues. You will find improvement opportunities and implement them in the network. If you’re looking to develop your career, you’ll be able to access development and mentorship opportunities to help you progress at Amazon.

Based at one of our sites, you'll be where the action is and work in an environment that encourages collaboration with a variety of internal and external stakeholders. By tapping into the latest performance indicators and metrics, you'll help us unlock our network's full potential.

Basic Qualifications


Experience in data analysis
Speak, write, and read fluently in English
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel


Preferred Qualifications


Ability to work in a team environment
Problem solving experience
Ability to work independently
Experience with basic data analysis and reporting
Knowledge of Amazon systems and processes
Strong organizational skills
Interest in learning new technical tools
Basic understanding of data visualization concepts
Ability to maintain attention to detail
Good communication skills


Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
About Us

With over 185 years of history, we are a leading health and wellness company. Our portfolio includes iconic brands such as Aquilea, Fisiocrem, Laborest, Sidroga, Biodramina, Aerored, Fave de Fuca, Halibut, Ems, and Depuralina, with a strong presence across major European markets.

We believe in the power of nature, enhanced by science, as the best way to improve human health and wellbeing sustainably. Our goal is to create a positive impact on both people's lives and the planet. We are currently seeking a highly motivated and skilled Data Analyst to join our team.




Your Mission

We are currently looking to strengthen our Data team by appointing a Data Analyst who will play a key role in designing and delivering scalable, high-quality reporting solutions across the organization. As part of the Data Department, you will work cross-functionally with business stakeholders and Data Engineering, acting as a trusted partner between data and decision-making. You will lead end-to-end analytics projects, drive best practices, and contribute to the continuous evolution of our data platform while mentoring more junior team members.




Key Responsibilities

In this role, you will be expected to:

Lead end-to-end reporting projects autonomously, from requirements gathering and data modeling to development, validation, and deployment in Power BI
Design and maintain optimized semantic data models (e.g., star schema, reusable DAX measures) to support multiple business use cases
Act as an analytical business partner, proactively identifying insights, KPIs, and opportunities beyond initial stakeholder requests
Ensure data quality, consistency, and performance of dashboards, troubleshooting and optimizing complex models when needed
Collaborate closely with Data Engineering teams to define and improve data models and architectures aligned with reporting needs
Drive reporting automation, documentation standards, and support/mentor business users and junior analysts in dashboard usage and best practices




You'll excel if you have...

3–5 years of experience in Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or similar roles, with at least 2 years of hands-on experience working intensively with Power BI in production environments
Advanced expertise in Power BI, including semantic modeling, intermediate-advanced DAX (time intelligence, filter context, CALCULATE, variables), Power Query (M), and performance optimization
Strong SQL skills (complex queries with multiple joins, CTEs, window functions, subqueries, and advanced aggregations), combined with solid knowledge of dimensional data modeling (star schema, SCDs, granularity)
Experience designing reports for different audiences (operational to executive), applying best practices in data visualization and storytelling
Strong ownership and autonomy, with the ability to lead projects end-to-end, manage multiple priorities, and ensure high-quality outputs with rigorous attention to detail
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to challenge requirements and translate business needs into scalable analytical solutions
A proactive, curious, and improvement-driven mindset, with experience mentoring junior profiles and collaborating across technical and non-technical teams
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks), Medallion architectures (Bronze/Silver/Gold), version control (Git), or DataOps practices is a plus
Experience with other BI tools (Tableau, Looker, Qlik), systems such as SAP/Salesforce/Odoo, or programming in Python for data analysis is considered an advantage
Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Business Administration, Economics, or equivalent; relevant certifications (e.g., PL-300, DP-600) are a plus
Fluent English (minimum B2 spoken) and Spanish, additional languages are a plus

Title: Data Analyst Consultant
Description:
En Prenomics ayudamos a nuestros clientes a tomar mejores decisiones gracias a sus datos. Buscamos un perfil capaz de entender el negocio, explorar la complejidad del dato y construir soluciones end-to-end que generen impacto tangible — desde la integración y curación de datos hasta modelos predictivos y visualizaciones con herramientas de BI. Todo ello en contacto directo con el cliente.


Tareas



Análisis del uso que hace el cliente de los datos y estratégia de negocio.


Detección de oportunidades de explotación de sus datos y de datos externos con alto impacto en su negocio.


Exploración y análisis de datos, identificación de variables relevantes para resolver retos concretos.


Estudio y modelización de la estructura global de datos del cliente.


Construcción de soluciones a problemas de negocio en base a datos de principio a fin (compresión de necesidad, integrción del dato, curación de datos, visualización con herramientas de business intelligence, construcción de algoritmos y modelos predictivos).


Comunicación de resultados e interacción directa con clientes.



Requisitos



Titulación: Grado en Matemáticas, Física, Ingeniería, Química. Máster en BI, Business Analytics, Data Science, o experiencia equivalente.


Herramientas: SQL, Python


Nivel de Español hablado y escrito nativo.


Sensibilidad de negocio y pensamiento estratégico.


Fuertes habilidades analíticas


Capacidad de comunicación y presentación de resultados analíticos



Beneficios



Participar en proyectos innovadores en el ámbito del Data Science (nuestra tecnología combina BI con soluciones de machine learning adaptadas a cada cliente y con las que él mismo interacciona).


Contacto directo con los clientes y acceso a problemas reales a los que se enfrentan las empresas, diseñando soluciones con un alto impacto.


Formar parte de una startup en crecimiento y colaborar a diario con un equipo de expertos en consultoría estrategia, data science y desarrollo de software en la nube.


Trabajar con tecnologías de última generación





Beneficios Prenomics





Horario de trabajo flexible e híbrido


Suministro ilimitado de bebidas con y sin cafeína


Horas semanales dedicadas a apreder y discutir nuevas tecnologías


Sistema de retribución flexible: tickets restaurante y transporte


Evaluaciones de desempeño, diseño de plan de carrera y revisiones salariales anuales


Actividades de teambuilding con todo el equipo

Title: Associate Data Analyst
Description:
Ready to make an impact in the connected vehicle and telematics industry through data?

As an Associate Data Analyst at Webfleet, a Bridgestone company, you'll turn complex data into actionable insights that support business decisions. Working within the Marketing Strategy team, you'll analyze product, sales, revenue, pricing, customer, and usage data to create reports and dashboards that drive business performance.

You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams including data analysts, product managers, marketers, sales, customer success, and business managers to deliver reliable reporting and analytics.

Your day-to-day:

As an Associate Data Analyst, you are on the forefront driving business results by generating data-based insights.


Data mining and data analyses to generate new insights for well-informed business decisions with the stakeholder support.
Collect data from multiple sources and databases and consolidate it in a single data source to generate business reports (usually Power BI, but also Tableau, Salsforce reports, or simliar visualization tools)
Identify, analyze, and interpret trends in complex datasets.
Develop powerful reports and business dashboards with excellent user experience to take business decisions.
Steering efforts to exploit new data types and sources internally.
Project Management of business initiatives end-2-end. For example, prepare analytics but also prepare presentation to defend the topic in front of the business unit management, initially with the coach and support of a team lead.
Business Analysis Capabilities: Analytical Problem-Solving capabilities, Ability to conduct Interpersonal and consultative interactions, excellent oral and written communication.
Close collaboration with Line of Business Management, sales teams, customer success, Product Managers, data scientists, marketeers, data engineers and analysts to improve business performance on KPIs as customer upsell and churn, prospecting, and rich-data content generation.



Who are you?

There is no set route to become our new Associate Data Analyst. But to be successful in this role, this is the kind of profile we have in mind:


You have at least 3 years’ experience in building dashboards and handling data sets, including DAX, Power Query, data modelling, and Power BI Service (workspace management, report publishing, app publishing, and scheduled refreshes).
You can utilize several data sources, especially relational databases. This includes having knowledge in SQL.
You have experience visualizing and presenting data utilizing Power BI (Marketing Cloud Intelligence, Tableau, QlickView, are a plus).
You have knowledge in DAX Formulas, Power Query and , data modelling, and data visualization, since this will be part of the day to day job.
Be ready to be exposed to frameworks, standards, and software categories such as Azure or similar.
Personal must-haves: problem-solving skills, analytic mind, aim to learn, flexible thinking, accountability, and good communication skills.
Bachelors in science, Statistics, Engineering, Mathematics or Economics degree, with special focus on data analytics or and statistics.
Fluent in English. Additional language is a plus.
Would be a plus:
Experience with Salesforce or other CRM platforms, Mixpanel, and reporting tools.
Knowledge of statistics, Python (or another object-oriented programming language), and basic machine learning concepts.
Familiarity with Scrum or other Agile ways of working.
Ability to independently own projects and collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders.



Why you’ll love working for us:


The chance to work in a fast moving, innovative and international team, dealing with different countries and cultures;
A company culture that likes to work hard and play hard. We like to have fun. There is no sense coming to a job everyday if you don’t like the people you work with;
Decisive actions. We don’t like to wait. It’s in our DNA to move forward. So, it only makes sense that we don’t jam the company with lots of layers. We make things happen. Quickly.



Let’s not forget the good stuff!


Working from home up to 3 days a week.
500 euros gross to set up your home office and additional monthly home office allowance
Chance to work abroad for 40 working days per year
Birthday off and volunteering time off.
Additional health and dental insurance.
100% employer funded pension scheme.
Lunch, childcare or transportation vouchers.
Unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning.
Access to Gofluent platform to learn new languages.
Free access to CALM (top mental health app).
Employee Assistance Program.
Discount on Bridgestone tyres.



Who are we?

Webfleet is Bridgestone’s globally trusted fleet management solution. More than 50,000 businesses across the world use it to improve fleet efficiency, support drivers, boost safety, stay compliant and work more sustainably. For more than 25 years it has been empowering fleet managers with data-driven insights that help them optimize their operations.

Webfleet contributes towards the delivery of The Bridgestone E8 Commitment. This broad, global corporate commitment clearly defines the value Bridgestone is promising to deliver to society, customers and future generations in eight focus areas: Energy, Ecology, Efficiency, Extension, Economy, Emotion, Ease and Empowerment. These provide a compass to guide strategic priorities, decision making and actions throughout every area of the business.

More information at: webfleet.com. Follow us on X: @WebfleetNews and LinkedIn @Webfleet. For more information on Bridgestone corporation visit Bridgestone.com or the Bridgestone Newsroom.

Want to join us?

If that’s a yes, please apply and upload your CV (In English)

We are an equal-opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability status.

Title: Senior Data Analyst | Data & Insights | AI-Driven Analytics
Description:
🔹 Senior Data Analyst | Data & Insights | AI-Driven Analytics 🔹







📌 About the role




We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join a Data & Insights team within an international transformation environment.

In this role, you will play a key part in shaping how data supports decision-making across the organisation, working at the intersection of advanced analytics, business performance, and emerging AI capabilities.

You will contribute to high-impact initiatives across commercial performance, operational analytics, finance insights, and the evolution of self-service analytics and data products.

This is a highly business-facing role, ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex datasets into clear insights, building meaningful KPIs, supporting senior stakeholders, and exploring how AI can improve productivity, insight generation, and data discoverability.

If you enjoy working with SQL, Python, Power BI, large datasets, business stakeholders, and AI-driven analytics tools, this could be a great fit.







💻 What you’ll do




🔹 Deliver high-quality analytical insights to support strategic decision-making across commercial, operational, and finance domains

🔹 Translate complex data into clear, structured, and actionable outputs for business and senior stakeholders

🔹 Prepare reports, dashboards, and performance analysis suitable for senior management and executive-level decision-making

🔹 Work closely with business stakeholders to understand needs, define relevant KPIs, and build performance frameworks

🔹 Analyse business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational efficiency, customer behaviour, and commercial performance

🔹 Support and enhance existing reporting solutions, including Power BI dashboards and performance reports

🔹 Contribute to the design and evolution of self-service analytics solutions and data products

🔹 Collaborate with Data Engineers and platform teams to ensure data quality, consistency, and usability

🔹 Work with large datasets using SQL, Python, Power BI, and other analytical tools

🔹 Identify trends, patterns, risks, and opportunities through data analysis and business context

🔹 Explore and experiment with AI-driven tools and approaches to improve insight generation, productivity, and data accessibility

🔹 Help improve data adoption across the organisation by making insights clear, accessible, and actionable

🔹 Communicate complex analytical topics in a clear and structured way to both technical and non-technical audiences

🔹 Work in a fast-paced, transformation-driven environment with cross-functional teams







💡 Must Have




🔹 6+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Data & Insights, or similar roles

🔹 Strong experience working with large datasets and analytical tools

🔹 Strong proficiency in SQL

🔹 Experience with Python for data analysis, automation, or analytical workflows

🔹 Strong experience with Power BI or similar BI / data visualisation tools

🔹 Proven ability to turn complex data into actionable business insights

🔹 Experience building dashboards, reports, KPIs, and performance analysis for business users

🔹 Good understanding of business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational KPIs, commercial performance, or finance metrics

🔹 Experience working with stakeholders at different levels, including senior business stakeholders

🔹 Ability to communicate complex topics in a clear, structured, and business-oriented way

🔹 Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and attention to detail

🔹 Curiosity and willingness to experiment with new technologies, especially AI-driven analytics tools

🔹 Comfortable working in ambiguous, fast-moving, and transformation-driven environments

🔹 Intermediate English level or above







✨ Nice to Have




🔹 Experience preparing reporting for senior management, executive committees, or management committees

🔹 Experience contributing to self-service analytics, data products, or reusable reporting frameworks

🔹 Exposure to AI tools and technologies such as Generative AI, analytics copilots, ML tools, ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, or similar

🔹 Experience using AI tools to improve productivity, automate analysis, generate insights, or support data discoverability

🔹 Experience collaborating with Data Engineers, Analytics Engineers, or platform teams

🔹 Experience with data quality, data governance, data modelling, or analytics engineering practices

🔹 Experience with Tableau, Qlik, Looker, Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or similar tools

🔹 Background in commercial analytics, operational analytics, finance analytics, pricing, revenue management, or performance tracking







🏢 Hybrid model - 2 days on site per week







🌍 Why join this project?




🤝 People first – diverse and inclusive culture in an international environment.

📊 Work on high-impact Data & Insights initiatives across business performance, operations, finance, and commercial domains.

🚀 Help shape how data supports strategic decision-making across a large international organisation.

🧠 Explore the evolution of AI-driven analytics, productivity tools, and data discoverability.

⚙️ Contribute to self-service analytics, data products, and scalable reporting practices.

📈 Work closely with business and senior stakeholders, turning complex data into meaningful insights.

😁 High team stability and collaborative culture.

🎓 €1200 per year training budget and continuous learning opportunities.

💰 Flexible compensation model.

🩺 Private health insurance and benefits package.

⚡ Flexible working hours and hybrid model.

🏋️ Wellhub: fitness, wellness, and mental health support.

⚽ Football and paddle tennis teams sponsored by Capitole.

🥳 Team buildings, global events, and strong tech communities.







✨ Want to know more about us? Click here and discover all the details.




🔍 Curious about our culture? Check out what people are saying about us on Glassdoor.




💬 We know that not every candidate will meet 100% of the requirements. If your profile doesn’t match perfectly but you believe you can add value, we’d still love to hear from you.




👉 Ready for the challenge? Apply now!




Empowering People, Unlocking Innovation.




Information Security Notice

The employee will have access to confidential information related to Capitole and the assigned project.
Compliance with internal security and information protection policies is mandatory.
NDA signature required.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

We are looking for an exceptional Data Analyst to join the Coches.Net, Motos.Net and Milanuncios.com team. You will work as a member of multidisciplinary team, collaborating closely with Product owners, cluster teams, data engineers and management team stake-holders (Marketing, Sales, Product, BizDev and other Operations teams).

Your main mission is to define and execute analyses of several business context to identify trends, opportunities and problems to improve the offering and services we offer.

Job Requirements


3+ years of experience in eCommerce, an online company.
Advanced knowledge of SQL and relational databases
Advanced knowledge of Python and/or R for data analysis (e.g. pandas, numpy, matplotlib), and advanced analytics (e.g. scikit-learn)
Advanced user of at least one visualisation tool such as Tableau and/or QlikSense.
Analytical skills: know how to interpret data, to extract relevant insights and be able to translate business questions into analytical needs Nice-to-have
Experience working with sales, operations, and strategy teams.
Good knowledge of basic machine learning algorithms (e.g. clustering, classification, regressions)


Nice To Have


Experience working with sales, operations, and strategy teams.
Good knowledge of basic machine learning algorithms (e.g. clustering, classification, regressions)
Some experience with big data technology (e.g. databricks, datalakes)
Some knowledge of data engineering tools (e.g. Airflow, DBT)


Job Responsibilities


Identify, analyse and interpret KPIs and Health Metrics related to our users and customers, creating dashboard and visualisation to monitor them.
Identifying trends, risks and opportunities to provide insights that can make recommendations on how to improve the different initiatives of the vertical.
Create powerful Insights using Advanced analytics to improve the knowledge of our users and customers.
Actively participate in the discovery, and development of advanced analytical models (such as clustering, and predictive models), into their implementation in business use cases, such as customer segmentation, and propensity models for upselling, downselling, and churn prediction.

  • Actively participate in the discovery, and development of advanced analytical models (such as clustering, and predictive models), into their implementation in business use cases, such as customer segmentation, and propensity models for upselling, downselling, and churn prediction.

Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
We power people’s progress.

At Preply, we’re all about creating life-changing learning experiences. We help people discover the magic of the perfect tutor, craft a personalised learning journey, and stay motivated to keep growing. Our approach is human-led, tech-enabled - and it’s creating real impact.

We’ve just reached unicorn status with a $150M Series D, accelerating our vision to transform education through human-led, AI-enhanced learning. Today, 100,000+ tutors teach 90+ languages to learners in 180 countries - and we’re only getting started. As a category-defining company, we’re shaping what the future of learning looks like at global scale.

Every Preply lesson sparks change, fuels ambition, and drives progress that matters. Joining Preply means helping define the future of education at global scale, and building something that truly matters for millions of people, every day.

Meet the team!

At Preply, data is at the heart of every decision we make. We run hundreds of analysis to continually optimize our product, each with its own analytical and tracking challenges. The complexity of our subscription model, along with the unique dynamics of tutor-learner interactions, offers an exciting opportunity for those looking to make a real impact.

As a Staff Data Analyst , you will own the data strategy for a critical product domain (e.g., Growth, Retention, Marketplace) and act as the senior analytical authority in that space. You will partner with Directors and VPs across Business, Operations, Product, and Engineering to shape long-term strategy, influence investment decisions, and ensure our roadmap is grounded in rigorous, scalable measurement frameworks.

Our Data Team is dedicated to empowering top-quality decision-making. Do you want to know how? Visit our Tech Radar to learn about the technologies we use at Preply!

What you’ll be doing:


Build and own the analytical framework leadership uses to evaluate B2B trade-offs, and define the long-term measurement framework that identifies structural growth levers, not just incremental wins.
Lead retention and cohort analyses that answer specific open questions from B2B leadership, turning findings into concrete pricing, product, or lifecycle recommendations.
Own impact modeling and scenario analysis for major B2B product initiatives, feeding directly into quarterly and annual planning through forecasting, trade-off analysis, and risk assessment.
Own metric definitions and governance for B2B, including resolving conflicting definitions across teams and maintaining a single source of truth others are expected to use.
Design the experimentation and analytics agenda: what gets tested, how success is measured, and run the analysis that determines whether initiatives actually moved the needle.
Partner with Directors and VPs across product, ops, and engineering to shape B2B strategy, mentor senior analysts to raise the analytical bar, and occasionally extend that work beyond B2B when company-level questions require it.


What you need to succeed:


You can point to one or more specific decisions (a pricing change, a resourcing call, a roadmap cut) that happened because of your analysis, and one where your recommendation was rejected and you can explain why, in hindsight, that was right or wrong.
You've owned a metric or strategic framework other teams were required to use, and resolved a conflict between two teams who each wanted a different definition to win.
You understand subscription or marketplace economics well enough to build an LTV or cohort model from scratch, not just interpret one someone else built.
Fluent, independent SQL and Python. You are the person other analysts send their code to for review, not the person waiting on review.
You've said no to a stakeholder's request because it wasn't the right question, and you've been right to.
You've built a forecast or scenario model that a VP used to defend a bet to their own leadership.


Nice to have:


Background in B2B SaaS, enterprise or account-based business models.
Experience building or scaling an analytics function (hiring, tooling, process, KPI frameworks…)
Experience with Tableau, Looker, or Power BI.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field.


Why you’ll love it at Preply:


An open, collaborative, dynamic and diverse culture;
A generous monthly allowance for lessons on Preply.com, Learning & Development budget and time off for your self-development;
A competitive financial package with equity, leave allowance and health insurance;
Not in Barcelona? We offer an attractive relocation package to join us in our Preply Barcelona Hub
Access to free mental health support platforms;
Access to Gympass-partnered wellness and gym centers throughout Spain to promote and support well-being and physical health;
The opportunity to unlock the potential of learners and tutors through language learning and teaching in 175 countries (and counting!).


Our Principles


Care to change the world - We are passionate about our work and care deeply about its impact to be life changing.
We do it for learners - For both Preply and tutors, learners are why we do what we do. Every day we focus on empowering tutors to deliver an exceptional learning experience.
Keep perfecting - To create an outstanding customer experience, we focus on simplicity, smoothness, and enjoyment, continually perfecting it as every detail matters.
Now is the time - In a fast-paced world, it matters how quickly we act. Now is the time to make great things happen.
Disciplined execution - What makes us disciplined is the excellence in our execution. We set clear goals, focus on what matters, and utilize our resources efficiently.
Dive deep - We leverage business acumen and curiosity to investigate disparities between numbers and stories, unlocking meaningful insights to guide our decisions.
Growth mindset - We proactively seek growth opportunities and believe today's best performance becomes tomorrow's starting point. We humbly embrace feedback and learn from setbacks.
Raise the bar - We raise our performance standards continuously, alongside each new hire and promotion. We build diverse and high-performing teams that can make a real difference.
Challenge, disagree and commit - We value open and candid communication, even when we don’t fully agree. We speak our minds, challenge when necessary, and fully commit to decisions once made.
One Preply - We prioritize collaboration, inclusion, and the success of our team over personal ambitions. Together, we support and celebrate each other's progress.


Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

Preply.com is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people of diverse backgrounds can thrive. We believe that the presence of different opinions and viewpoints is a key ingredient for our success as a multicultural Ed-Tech company. That means that Preply will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age or veteran status.

Description:
Barcelona, Spain
Finance
Permanent - Full Time

Data Administration Analyst

Apply Now

Share

Job ID: r20830

What You'll Do:

As Vendor Master Data / T&E Specialist, you will be responsible for:


the reliability and quality of the Vendor Master Data for all Criteo’s entities worldwide.
the quality, process and cut off expense claims for all central entities
Ensuring and fulfill our KYS process, SOX standards and answering to our periodic audit controls queries


This requires close collaboration with the IT, GL Tax, Procurement and Internal Control teams, as well as with the Accounts Payable team in Paris and other members of the finance team in the complex countries. You will be part of Finance Shared Services and will report to the VMD/T&E Team Lead, based in Paris.

Your Tasks Will Include:


Vendor Master Data
Ensuring vendors are created, updated and deleted appropriately, and in compliance with SOX standards
Ensuring Vendor data is completed in alignment with contracts signed by the Business Development, Procurement and any Purchasing requester
Cleaning, upgrading, updating and due diligence of the vendor master database with our screening compliance tool as regard of the KYS process
T&E process
Ensuring that all T&E are processed in a timely manner in compliance with SOX requirements
Process and manage company card expenses and queries
Identifying gaps and escalating issues
Service quality
Ensuring that queries are answered in a timely manner


Who You Are:


Highly Organized and Efficient, Structured, with good analytical skills
Communication skills are key – good team spirit
Fluent in English
Previous experience on SAP is a plus
Experience of working in a fast-moving international environment
Results driven
Internal customer satisfaction oriented
Problem solving


We acknowledge that many candidates may not meet every single role requirement listed above. If your experience looks a little different from our requirements but you believe that you can still bring value to the role, we’d love to see your application!

Who We Are:

We’re Criteo, the Commerce Intelligence Platform. Criteo helps businesses turn shopper signals into commerce outcomes while delivering more relevant experiences for shoppers. We use proprietary commerce intelligence and AI decisioning to drive relevance for shoppers and performance for businesses.

At Criteo, our culture is as unique as it is diverse. From our offices across the globe or from the comfort of home, our 3,600 Criteos collaborate together to build an open, impactful, and forward-thinking environment.

We foster a workplace where everyone is valued, and employment decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and business needs—never on non-job-related factors or legally protected characteristics.

What We Offer:

🏢 Ways of working – Our hybrid model blends home with in-office experiences, making space for both.

📈 Grow with us – Learning, mentorship & career development programs.

💪 Your wellbeing matters – Health benefits, wellness perks & mental health support.

🤝 A team that cares – Diverse, inclusive, and globally connected.

💸 Fair pay & perks – Attractive salary, with performance-based rewards and family-friendly policies, plus the potential for equity depending on role and level.

Additional benefits may vary depending on the country where you work and the nature of your employment with Criteo.

Perks & Benefits for all Criteos.

Happy people make healthy teams, so we value employee well-being above all else. Discover the perks & benefits we offer all our Criteos!!

Ways of Working

Trust and flexibility are at our core, so is the power of being together. Our hybrid model blends empowerment with shared in-person experiences, making space for both. We believe this is the most valuable way to foster collaboration in a way that helps us grow, together.

View more

Learning & Career Development

As your skills multiply, opportunities do too. So, we care to help you nurture your potential, identify new goals, and define what you want out of your career. Discover how we have diversified our learning resources and training catalog for you to shape greater opportunities. You care for your career growth, and we do too.

View more

Corporate Social Responsibility

We care to build an open future for our community and beyond. Discover how our people fuel our Corporate Social Responsibility from the bottom up, embracing but also embodying and promoting values of diversity, equity, and inclusion through everything they do. Together, we strive to build a future that truly includes everyone.

View more

Everything you need to know about our culture, vision, values, and how we live them with people, both in and out of Criteo, is in our Culture Book. Discover who we are, what we do, and how we act as a company and as Criteos!

Our culture

Criteo came to spring off of a few great minds, back in 2005. In Spain, we welcome our Criteos in our bustling office in Barcelona and our long-thriving virtual hub of Madrid. Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción de la empresa

MSX International es un líder global en servicios para el sector de automoción, presente en más de 80 países. Desde nuestra sede en Barcelona trabajamos con diferentes fabricantes internacionales para ofrecer soluciones innovadoras que impulsan la eficiencia, la calidad y el rendimiento de sus operaciones.

Si buscas un entorno dinámico, analítico, internacional y orientado a la mejora continua, este es tu sitio.

Descripción del empleo

Como Analista de Datos, serás responsable de gestionar, analizar y transformar datos clave relacionados con proyectos del sector automoción. Tu misión será convertir grandes volúmenes de datos en insights claros y accionables que apoyen la toma de decisiones, la optimización de procesos y el rendimiento general del negocio.

Requisitos


Realizar análisis diarios, semanales y mensuales utilizando herramientas como Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Identificar tendencias, variaciones y oportunidades de mejora
Preparar informes avanzados alineados con KPIs y requisitos del proyecto
Mantener, actualizar y asegurar la calidad de la documentación y reportes en herramientas como SharePoint y Office365
Identificar patrones en los datos y proponer acciones correctivas u oportunidades de optimización
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para convertir insights en acciones concretas
Garantizar el cumplimiento de estándares internos en informes y dashboards
Presentar análisis, conclusiones e información relevante a equipos internos y dirección
Asegurar la precisión, consistencia y calidad de los datos analizados
Proponer mejoras continuas en procesos, metodologías y flujos de análisis


Requisitos

Conocimientos y habilidades


Habilidades de comunicación y capacidad para trabajar con equipos diversos
Enfoque resolutivo para problemas complejos
Capacidad para gestionar prioridades y trabajar con plazos ajustados
Dominio de Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Conocimientos de sistemas de gestión de datos, minería de datos, procesos ETL y reporting automatizado
Competencias en análisis estadístico y modelos de rendimiento
Experiencia con herramientas de automatización (RPA) es un plus
Conocimiento de Microsoft Dynamics (deseable)


Formación

Como Analista de Datos, serás responsable de gestionar, analizar y transformar datos clave relacionados con proyectos del sector automoción. Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Overview

Keysight is at the forefront of technology innovation, delivering breakthroughs and trusted insights in electronic design, simulation, prototyping, test, manufacturing, and optimization. Our ~16,800 employees create world-class solutions in communications, 5G, automotive, energy, quantum, aerospace, defense, and semiconductor markets for customers in over 100 countries. Learn more about what we do.

Our award-winning culture embraces a bold vision of where technology can take us and a passion for tackling challenging problems with industry-first solutions. We believe that when people feel a sense of belonging, they can be more creative, innovative, and thrive at all points in their careers.

Responsibilities

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Customer Master Data & Business Analyst Intern to join our team in Barcelona. This internship offers hands-on experience working with business data, customer data management, reporting, pricing analytics, process improvement, and data-driven decision-making while collaborating with cross-functional teams across a global organization.

Key Responsibilities


Assist in collecting, cleaning, validating, and maintaining customer and business data from multiple sources.
Perform exploratory data analysis to identify trends, patterns, business insights, and opportunities for process improvement.
Create dashboards, reports, KPIs, and data visualizations for internal stakeholders and business leaders.
Support ongoing analytics, reporting, pricing, and customer master data projects, as well as ad-hoc data requests.
Help automate recurring reports and improve data management and business processes.
Support quotation analysis, reporting activities, and approval workflows.
Collaborate with global teams to ensure data accuracy, consistency, and operational efficiency.
Document methodologies, business processes, assumptions, and data definitions.


Qualifications

Must Have


Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Marketing, Information Technology, Engineering, Data Analytics, or a related field, with the ability to sign a full-time 12-month internship agreement through the university.
Basic knowledge of data analysis concepts, reporting, and statistical methods.
Experience with Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Attention to detail and a structured approach to work.
Strong communication skills in English.
Ability to work effectively in a collaborative and international environment.


Nice to Have


Experience with data visualization and business intelligence tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Looker).
Basic knowledge of databases, SQL, and data modeling.
Experience creating reports, dashboards, or KPI tracking.
Interest in customer data management, pricing, business operations, or process improvement.


Careers Privacy Statement***Keysight is an Equal Opportunity Employer.***

Title: Data & Yield Analyst
Description:
About Softonic

At Softonic, people come first. We’re a multicultural team passionate about building digital experiences used by millions of users worldwide. Guided by a fail fast, learn faster mindset, we encourage curiosity, experimentation, and continuous improvement, to make a real impact.

Our culture is shaped by four principles:


Step up: Take ownership and act with integrity.
Work smart: Focus on impact and efficiency.
One team: Collaborate openly and support each other.
Think big: Stay curious and challenge the status quo.


Team up with us to grow your career in an international, collaborative, and people-focused company where your ideas matter and your work reaches a global audience.

About The Role

We are a global premium publisher with 28 years of history, providing quality software & APPs download services across multiple languages, also expanding with white labels (Download.com Filehippo), Publisher network (rev.amp), and more partnerships on the way.

As our data & yield analyst reporting to the Director of Advertising, you will be responsible for bridging all revenue team’s data-analytics need (including but not limited to datasets, data visualization, and automation with the process for revenue generation/optimization purposes) with our BI team, to drive initiatives that will boost making more & better data-driven decisions and improve efficiency on revenue generation. Will also take the yield function to analyze market trends & discover business opportunities, not only providing insights to the relevant teams (Sales, Paid, Partnership, Adtech, Product, Traffic, etc) but also following through with the implementation of the initiatives to ensure the business impact is realized.

Key Responsibilities


Be a data champion inside the Advertising & RevOps team and the go-to-person for ithe team & stakeholders from other revenue teams when it comes to better utilization of our data.
Help revenue teams to identify opportunities: using new data points, visualization and automation solutions and ideas to improve revenue generation efficiency, take those requirements, coordinate with dev (BI or other teams) to build the roadmap for the projects & implement accordingly.
Oversee & analyze mid-long (monthly, quarterly, YoY) term yield performance/trends of revenue streams & devices, provide insights on fluctuation, alerts when needed, and recommend a clear plan of action.
Provide A/B test or lean test analysis with recommendations for revenue-related initiatives.
Manage UPR optimisation and automation of pricing recommendation tool (either 3rd party or in-house).
Be able to use agentic AI tools to automate process & streamline data processing & analytic flows to deliver results efficiently
Provide insights on market trends, opportunities, and inventory availability information to the sales & AMs team for better usage of market data
Help teams turn data into actionable insights that unlock revenue opportunities and support smarter business decisions.


Must have


At least 2 years of working experience working as business analyst, preferably with experience in digital advertising business.
Experience of working with BI platforms, like Tableau, Qlikview & advance skills of Google Analytics.
Experience of doing data analysis work with AI tools like Claude, Gemini, OpenAI, Copilot, etc.
Experience of working with BigQuery, SQL & Python.
Preferable background on big data, data science related topics.
Experience building analytical models and working with structured and unstructured data sets to drive business decisions.
Knowledge of the online advertising ecosystem.
Basic understanding of budgeting, financial forecast, and accounting principles is preferred.
Excellent communication and project management skills with a strong business acumen.
Ability to work on own initiative in a fast-moving environment.
High level of motivation, determination and commitment.
An excellent command of both written and spoken English is required.


Why Softonic?


Private Health Care
Hybrid Work Model (3 days on-site per week in Poblenou area)
Flexible remote work options during certain periods of the year
Flexible Spending Plan & Free Tax Consultant
Learning & Development: Language Classes (English, Spanish, Catalan) and Training Program
Workplace Amenities: Dining Room, Gaming Room, Terraces, and complimentary fresh fruit, coffee, snacks, and drinks
Team & Social Events: Pizza & Beers every Thursday
Lifestyle Benefits: Discounts at gyms and restaurants
Additional Time Off: Birthday leave
Opt for: Mac or Windows laptop
Wellhub program


Softonic is committed to equal opportunity and does not discriminate on the basis of race, religious beliefs, color, gender, age, citizenship, origins, disability, sexual orientation, sexual identity, background, marital status, or any other characteristic. If you need any specific accommodation/adaptation due to a disability during the recruitment process, please send us an email to recruitment@softonic.com with your request and we'll contact you.

You are welcome to share your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her, he/him, they/them).

Title: Fraud Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

Satispay began by rethinking the simple act of a payment to remove the friction from our daily routines. But we didn't stop there. Today, we are building a complete financial platform designed to empower people and concretely improve their lives.

By giving our 6 million users a clear, open path to pay, save, and invest, we are evolving into the definitive destination for every financial need.

What You'll Be Doing

As our Fraud Data Analyst, you'll be the person who secures the future of digital payments by architecting sophisticated real-time defense strategies.

Here's What Your Day-to-day Will Look Like


Architect Real-Time Strategies – You will be responsible for our real-time decisioning engine, designing and deploying logic that combines ML scores with velocity features to make millisecond decisions.
Precision Rule Development – Translate complex fraud patterns into high-performing rules, optimizing the balance between False Positive Rates (FPR) and Gross Fraud Loss.
Deconstruct Payment Fraud – Proactively hunt for emerging schemes (ATO, social engineering) and turn these insights into end-to-end mitigation strategies.
Optimize the Decisioning Stack – Partner with Product and Engineering to define analytical requirements for new features and third-party data signals.
Calibrate ML & Heuristics – Work with Data Science to operationalize machine learning models, determining how to best "wrap" model scores with heuristic rules.
Champion Frictionless UX – Analyze security measures to identify unnecessary hurdles, designing dynamic friction strategies for a seamless user experience.
Strategic Performance Reporting – Monitor rule efficacy and portfolio health, presenting data-driven recommendations to senior leadership to evolve our risk appetite.


Expect challenges, collaboration, and the freedom to bring your ideas to life in a fast-moving and supportive environment. Things change quickly here, so be ready to adapt, take initiative, and shape your role as we grow.

Who We're Looking For

We need a problem-solver who loves teamwork and gets things done. If you're curious and ready for real ownership, you'll fit in! Does this sound like you?


Experience – You have between 3 and 7 years of professional experience, specifically within the risk, payments, or credit sectors.
Technical Proficiency – You are highly skilled in SQL and Python for data analysis and strategy development.
Domain Expertise – You have a solid understanding of payment fraud dynamics and experience working with real-time decisioning environments.
Communication – You possess excellent communication skills and are fluent in English.
Nice to Have – Experience with Machine Learning, Data Science, PySpark, or GraphQL knowledge is a significant plus.


CareAbout: how we support your impact

About

We move fast, and evolution never stops. It's a fun ride, but it can be challenging. To make sure our people truly thrive, we're committed to making their lives easier, both in the office and out in the world. That's why we created CareAbout:


❤️ Health (Private insurance for you and your family, psychological support with Serenis, mental health workshops)
💰 Financial resources (Stock Option Plan, Meal vouchers, Relocation support if you're moving countries)
⚙️ Growth and development (Professional development programs, Internal mobility, Language courses with Preply)
🌱 Flexibility (Unlimited PTO, Hybrid working policy*, Flexible working hours)
👨‍👩‍👧 Family (Enhanced parental leave, Additional leave for child sickness)
We embrace three days per week in-office (Tuesday and Thursday + 1 of your choice), with the option to request extra remote time.


Equal opportunity employer

At Satispay, we're proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and inclusion, welcoming individuals of all backgrounds. This opportunity is open to everyone, regardless - for instance - of race, colour, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, and national origin. Join us in a workplace where everyone belongs!

Learn More About Us

Our values and pillars aren't just fancy words on a page - they really shape everything we do. Explore them here.

By submitting this application, I acknowledge that I have read and understood the content of the Privacy Policy

Title: Sr Regional Operations Analyst
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

Join us and become a key member of Glovo's operations team! We offer a challenging and dynamic environment with many opportunities for learning and development.

The couriers are the key player in Glovo’s marketplace. We are looking for Operations Senior Analysts who will find the balance between creativity and data-driven objectivity in such a way that they carry out projects that seem impossible until they come true.

You will be in charge of providing & analyzing data to the team. Through analytics you will ensure we always have the right strategic data in place in order to take the best operation decisions having direct impact on the Local P&L.

Be a Part Of a Team Where You Will


Provide analytical support to the team throughout deeper understanding of Glovo's operations model
Perform analysis to come up with insights to help improve KPIs
Understand and fix data reliability issues
Act as the main data POC for the department supporting with data, reporting and adopting a data driven approach
Help to find insights through data analysis during the couriers first deliveries in order to maximize retention
Own dashboards and build new tools to improve the visibility of metrics in the department
Develop analytical frameworks and models for the area and be able to instruct and communicate for all the department
Understand and deep-dive into the data to understand the reasons and the levers we can act upon to improve KPIs (and be accountable for them)
Evaluate strategic opportunities, performing a broad range of quantitative analyses and A/B tests
Understand local needs, and put yourself in the skin of the courier - understand who they are.
Work closely with local/global stakeholders, product teams and development teams, serving as a bridge between product and operations, to define how to best set up and enable products, monitor and give visibility of metrics and KPIs.
Deep-dive on business metrics, in order to explore areas of opportunity and enable data-driven decision-making, based on applied statistics and probability techniques
Build optimization processes to define the best tuning of every feature on each geography.


Qualifications

YOU HAVE:


Passion about analytics: you enjoy deep diving into problems to find the root cause
Problem-solving skills: no problem is too small or too big
Excel / Google Sheets proficiency
Proficient in SQL (Python will be considered as plus)
Knowledge of visualization tools like Tableau, Looker, Data Studio & Qlikview
Exceptional verbal and written communication skills
Proactive, organized, and obsessed with details
University degree in a relevant field (economics, mathematics, organizational sciences and similar).
2+ years of experience in similar roles. Experience in Logistics would be a plus.
Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize workload, and handle a fast-paced environment.
Ability to work both independently and within a team.
Flexibility to travel to countries within the region
Capable of executing tasks quickly while maintaining a high standard of quality.
Very Hands-on, willingness to get hands dirty, go on the field and support all verticals of the company.
Experience in KPIs tracking and creation and optimization expertise


Additional Information

Individuals representing diverse profiles, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves

Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

Join us and become a key member of Glovo's operations team! We offer a challenging and dynamic environment with many opportunities for learning and development.

The couriers are the key player in Glovo’s marketplace. We are looking for Operations Senior Analysts who will find the balance between creativity and data-driven objectivity in such a way that they carry out projects that seem impossible until they come true.

You will be in charge of providing & analyzing data to the team. Through analytics you will ensure we always have the right strategic data in place in order to take the best operation decisions having direct impact on the Local P&L.

Be a Part Of a Team Where You Will

  • Provide analytical support to the team throughout deeper understanding of Glovo's operations model
  • Perform analysis to come up with insights to help improve KPIs
  • Understand and fix data reliability issues
  • Act as the main data POC for the department supporting with data, reporting and adopting a data driven approach
  • Help to find insights through data analysis during the couriers first deliveries in order to maximize retention
  • Own dashboards and build new tools to improve the visibility of metrics in the department
  • Develop analytical frameworks and models for the area and be able to instruct and communicate for all the department
  • Understand and deep-dive into the data to understand the reasons and the levers we can act upon to improve KPIs (and be accountable for them)
  • Evaluate strategic opportunities, performing a broad range of quantitative analyses and A/B tests
  • Understand local needs, and put yourself in the skin of the courier - understand who they are.
  • Work closely with local/global stakeholders, product teams and development teams, serving as a bridge between product and operations, to define how to best set up and enable products, monitor and give visibility of metrics and KPIs.
  • Deep-dive on business metrics, in order to explore areas of opportunity and enable data-driven decision-making, based on applied statistics and probability techniques
  • Build optimization processes to define the best tuning of every feature on each geography.

Qualifications

YOU HAVE:

  • Passion about analytics: you enjoy deep diving into problems to find the root cause
  • Problem-solving skills: no problem is too small or too big
  • Excel / Google Sheets proficiency
  • Proficient in SQL (Python will be considered as plus)
  • Knowledge of visualization tools like Tableau, Looker, Data Studio & Qlikview
  • Exceptional verbal and written communication skills
  • Proactive, organized, and obsessed with details
  • University degree in a relevant field (economics, mathematics, organizational sciences and similar).
  • 2+ years of experience in similar roles. Experience in Logistics would be a plus.
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize workload, and handle a fast-paced environment.
  • Ability to work both independently and within a team.
  • Flexibility to travel to countries within the region
  • Capable of executing tasks quickly while maintaining a high standard of quality.
  • Very Hands-on, willingness to get hands dirty, go on the field and support all verticals of the company.
  • Experience in KPIs tracking and creation and optimization expertise

Additional Information

Individuals representing diverse profiles, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Title: Revenue Management Analyst
Description:
Overview

Revenue Management Analyst

Design and implement the Pack, Price and Promotion Strategy across the different channels, with the objective of maximizing PepsiCo’s Net Revenue & Profitability.

This role sits at the intersection of consumer & commercial strategy, and financial performance, ensuring the right value propositions reach the right consumers, through the right channels, at the right price and in the right formats, while balancing profitability, competitiveness, and customer needs.

This role must have close collaboration with Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Category teams to drive sustainable growth through data-driven decision-making.

Responsibilities

Main Accountabilities:


Design and recommend Pack & Price strategies across brands and different sub-channels, grounded in insights and performance analysis
Lead Promotional strategy definition and implementation. Define the annual promo calendar, based on shopper insights to maximize performance. Perform periodic analysis to understand results & tailor the strategy.
Drive mix development initiatives to maximize Net Revenue Realization (NRR), acting as a category-wide owner of mix levers across channels and departments.
Own PPA initiatives, working together with sales, marketing & commercialization, ensuring market-fit and strategic relevance.
Lead the AOP strategy & algorithm, aligning short- and long-term initiatives to deliver volume, revenue, and profitability targets.
Create x-channel commercial policy, and define list prices for all SKUs, participating in the innovation gates.
Partner with Sales to co-create commercially sound proposals, including multipacks , bonuspacks or I&Os in order to boost volumes.
Support the implementation & adoption RGM Digital tools (e.g. elasticity, ROI, Uplift models…)
Anticipate proactively the market changes, for analysis and decision making (VAT or Tax impacts, legislations, sustainability goals..etc)
Monitor and track implemented initiatives in order to obtain learnings & insights to optimize and define future strategies.
Ensure cross-functional alignment across Sales, Trade Marketing, Insights, Finance, Marketing, Category Management, and Commercialization.


Qualifications

Experience required


At least 7 years experience in Revenue Management, Finance Planning, Insights, Commercial roles or similar.
Experience in the use of internal & external data bases (Nielsen, SAP,…)
Knowledge of RGM & finance KPIs (NR, MC, Elasticity, ROI..) preferred
Proactivity, ability to work in a cross functional environment and with collaboration spirit
Strong Microsoft Office skills (Excel and ppt)
Fluent in Spanish and English (advanced level)


Competencies Required


Good story telling and communication skills
Very strong analytical skills & high business knowledge as a broad perspective is needed to identify channel opportunities
Strategic thinking. Ability to see connection in data, anticipate potential risks/opportunities, and optimize & simplify processes.
Self-driven, able to work independently and take initiative
Team player & Proactive collaboration
Resilience and adaptability to change
Capacity to work under pressure and tight deadlines
Experience in stakeholder management


Why PepsiCo Iberia?

PepsiCo Iberia, part of the Western Europe Operating Unit, is home to a team of nearly 3,000 talented employees across our Barcelona, Madrid, Vitoria and Lisbon headquarters, and our production sites in Spain and Portugal.

With iconic brands like Pepsi, 7UP, Alvalle, Lay’s or Doritos, we continue to shape the food and beverage landscape as one of the region’s leading companies.

At PepsiCo, you’re not just joining a business - you’re joining a culture that champions innovation, encourages bold thinking and empowers every individual to make an impact.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About The Team

Kiloverse is the operational force that helps ventures grow — building the systems, teams, and infrastructure that turn ideas into scalable businesses. Behind every fast-growing product, there’s a group of people who think like owners, act fast, and execute with precision.

Here, you’ll find people who want to break big in their careers — working side by side with entrepreneurs and founders to build ventures that redefine the health, wellness, beauty, or travel industries.

Data team, as part of Kiloverse, the operational force behind every impactful Kilo solution, provides advanced data engineering, modelling and reporting solutions for the whole group.

Currently, we are looking for an experienced data analyst to work on internal media buying tool that will help scale the business even further.

Get ready to


Identify, investigate and solve data issues: data quality, data discrepancies, missing data
Contribute to the development and improvement of data solutions
Leverage the power of dbt for overcoming complex modelling problems with a focus on performance, robustness and scalability
Employ GCP tools (dbt, Airflow and Looker) to enhance data quality, efficiency, and delivery of accurate and timely data
Work on prevention and alerting solutions
Adopt and refine our best practices e.g. naming convention, data modeling, and data quality testing
Communicate with cross-functional teams and non-technical stakeholders in a clear and structured manner
Assist and support other team members in the design, development, and implementation of data warehousing, reporting, and analytics solutions
Take ownership of tasks and initiatives


We expect you to


Have at least 4 years of proven SQL skills: ability to join and manipulate data of various types (String, Integer, JSON, Array), write parameterized scripts, debug SQL code
Experience working with dbt is a plus
Experience with analytics tracking tools (e.g., Snowplow, GA4) is a strong plus
Know ETL and warehousing concepts
Have strong communication skills: timely, clear, and consistent sharing of information, work progress, bottlenecks and findings
Have curious and growth-oriented mindset towards constant learning
Be capable to understand, tackle, and communicate problems from both technical and business perspectives
Be fluent in English.


Salary

Gross salary range is 4000-5200 EUR/month.

Location

Choose between our Vilnius headquarters or our Kaunas hub — two great spaces designed for doing your best work.

For Vilnius: 4 days onsite, 1 flexible day remote. Meanwhile, in Kaunas: onsite, hybrid, or remote options are available depending on the role.

Speaking of perks:

Speaking of perks:

Own your wellness

7 extra days off, 3 for weddings, hybrid work, and the freedom to work 2 months from anywhere in the world.

Shape your growth path

Builders never stop learning. Coaching, mentorship, training, conferences, online courses, subscriptions, books – you’ve got a €1100 yearly budget to fuel personal or team growth. You pick the path – we’ll back you all the way.

Drive global impact

This is where ambition scales. Build impact across 30+ brands worldwide. Launch bold projects. Even step in as a co-founder – we reward those who push further. And if you bring in more great people, you get €1500 for every successful referral.


additional conditions apply based on your residence location.

Title: Compliance Expert Data Analyst
Description:
Our mission and customers: We are creating the freedom for SMEs to succeed by delivering Europe's leading finance workspace with banking at its core, augmented by financial tools. We are proud to be rated 4.8 on Trustpilot, based on 55,000+ reviews. Our culture puts customer satisfaction at the core of what we do, as proven by our Net Promoter Score of 75 (more about our culture here).

Our journey: Founded in 2017 by Alexandre and Steve, Qonto has grown to 1,600+ Qontoers serving over 600,000+ customers across 8 European countries. We have been profitable since 2023, and we are just getting started.

Our beliefs: We hire for skills and potential. With 80+ nationalities, 45% women, of which 56% of women in our leadership team, diversity isn't a program; It's who we are. We've built a discrimination-free hiring process because the best teams are built on merit.

AI at Qonto: AI is deeply embedded in how we work (here) - Every Qontoer gets unlimited access to the best AI tools. We want people who experiment without waiting for permission, push AI beyond the obvious, know when to trust it, and when to question it.

Join us as a Compliance Expert Data Analyst to build the analytics foundations that power Qonto's compliance execution. You'll own a brand-new scope — monitoring KYC/KYB funnels, remediation campaigns, and financial exposure — working cross-functionally with Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, and Internal Control, under the guidance of Alma, our Lead Data Analyst.

👩‍💻🧑‍💻 As a Data Analyst at Qonto, you will


Build compliance process monitoring from scratch: You'll build and own the data infrastructure that powers compliance monitoring at Qonto — from onboarding funnel analytics to remediation financial exposure — giving product, operations and compliance teams the self-service tools they need to make decisions without waiting on a data request.
Model and transform data with dbt: With the collaboration of Analytics Engineer Team, you'll build end-to-end transformation pipelines from source to consumption layer, keeping metric definitions clean and trusted by non-technical stakeholders.
Turn regulatory questions into self-service assets: You'll partner with Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, and Internal Control to scope and ship analytical tools
Own the KYC/KYB funnel analytics end-to-end: You'll deliver insights from CS initiation through KYB validation — refusal reasons, document gaps, lead-time distributions — so teams can act on friction fast.
Track remediation campaigns and financial exposure: You'll measure MRR secured, at risk, or lost across active campaigns, with peak intensity in Q4 (budget season) and May-June (reforecast).


🤔 What You Can Expect


A greenfield, strategically critical scope: Qonto is under close regulatory scrutiny — you'll build analytics foundations that matter company-wide, not just for your team.
Senior, cross-functional exposure from day one: Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, Internal Control — few DA roles give you this breadth of senior stakeholder access at this level.
Real ownership in the Modern Data Stack: You'll build in Omni and dbt with a high degree of autonomy — no legacy to untangle, just foundations to create.
A team where AI is a first-class tool: Unlimited access to AI tools (Claude, Cursor, and more) to evolve how you work, not just output more.


➡️ Your future manager

Your manager will be Alma, our Lead Data Analyst for Ops & Finance. Alma knows exactly what it takes to build an analytics function that actually changes decisions, and she'll push you to do the same.

Alma reports to Erwan, Director of Business Analytics who oversees the entire Analytics function and ensures alignment across teams.

🏅 About You


Compliance or regulated environment experience: You've worked analytically on KYC/KYB, AML, remediation, or similar regulatory/operational processes — and you know how to get credible with those stakeholders fast.
Self-service product mindset: You've designed dashboards and models for end-users to explore autonomously, adapting the same dataset from ops-level (daily SLA tracking) to exec-level (budget readouts).
Cross-functional stakeholder management: You've juggled competing analytical priorities across multiple teams — and you know how to scope clearly, push back when needed, and drive adoption.
Strong SQL and data modelling skills: You've built end-to-end transformation pipelines, enforced data quality, and made non-technical stakeholders trust your numbers.
AI-native workflow: You actively use AI tools to accelerate your analytics work — from data modelling to stakeholder communication — and you're genuinely curious about going further.

HTML:
Our mission and customers: We are creating the freedom for SMEs to succeed by delivering Europe's leading finance workspace with banking at its core, augmented by financial tools. We are proud to be rated 4.8 on Trustpilot, based on 55,000+ reviews. Our culture puts customer satisfaction at the core of what we do, as proven by our Net Promoter Score of 75 (more about our culture here).

Our journey: Founded in 2017 by Alexandre and Steve, Qonto has grown to 1,600+ Qontoers serving over 600,000+ customers across 8 European countries. We have been profitable since 2023, and we are just getting started.

Our beliefs: We hire for skills and potential. With 80+ nationalities, 45% women, of which 56% of women in our leadership team, diversity isn't a program; It's who we are. We've built a discrimination-free hiring process because the best teams are built on merit.

AI at Qonto: AI is deeply embedded in how we work (here) - Every Qontoer gets unlimited access to the best AI tools. We want people who experiment without waiting for permission, push AI beyond the obvious, know when to trust it, and when to question it.

Join us as a Compliance Expert Data Analyst to build the analytics foundations that power Qonto's compliance execution. You'll own a brand-new scope — monitoring KYC/KYB funnels, remediation campaigns, and financial exposure — working cross-functionally with Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, and Internal Control, under the guidance of Alma, our Lead Data Analyst.

👩‍💻🧑‍💻 As a Data Analyst at Qonto, you will

  • Build compliance process monitoring from scratch: You'll build and own the data infrastructure that powers compliance monitoring at Qonto — from onboarding funnel analytics to remediation financial exposure — giving product, operations and compliance teams the self-service tools they need to make decisions without waiting on a data request.
  • Model and transform data with dbt: With the collaboration of Analytics Engineer Team, you'll build end-to-end transformation pipelines from source to consumption layer, keeping metric definitions clean and trusted by non-technical stakeholders.
  • Turn regulatory questions into self-service assets: You'll partner with Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, and Internal Control to scope and ship analytical tools
  • Own the KYC/KYB funnel analytics end-to-end: You'll deliver insights from CS initiation through KYB validation — refusal reasons, document gaps, lead-time distributions — so teams can act on friction fast.
  • Track remediation campaigns and financial exposure: You'll measure MRR secured, at risk, or lost across active campaigns, with peak intensity in Q4 (budget season) and May-June (reforecast).

🤔 What You Can Expect

  • A greenfield, strategically critical scope: Qonto is under close regulatory scrutiny — you'll build analytics foundations that matter company-wide, not just for your team.
  • Senior, cross-functional exposure from day one: Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, Internal Control — few DA roles give you this breadth of senior stakeholder access at this level.
  • Real ownership in the Modern Data Stack: You'll build in Omni and dbt with a high degree of autonomy — no legacy to untangle, just foundations to create.
  • A team where AI is a first-class tool: Unlimited access to AI tools (Claude, Cursor, and more) to evolve how you work, not just output more.

➡️ Your future manager

Your manager will be Alma, our Lead Data Analyst for Ops & Finance. Alma knows exactly what it takes to build an analytics function that actually changes decisions, and she'll push you to do the same.

Alma reports to Erwan, Director of Business Analytics who oversees the entire Analytics function and ensures alignment across teams.

🏅 About You

  • Compliance or regulated environment experience: You've worked analytically on KYC/KYB, AML, remediation, or similar regulatory/operational processes — and you know how to get credible with those stakeholders fast.
  • Self-service product mindset: You've designed dashboards and models for end-users to explore autonomously, adapting the same dataset from ops-level (daily SLA tracking) to exec-level (budget readouts).
  • Cross-functional stakeholder management: You've juggled competing analytical priorities across multiple teams — and you know how to scope clearly, push back when needed, and drive adoption.
  • Strong SQL and data modelling skills: You've built end-to-end transformation pipelines, enforced data quality, and made non-technical stakeholders trust your numbers.
  • AI-native workflow: You actively use AI tools to accelerate your analytics work — from data modelling to stakeholder communication — and you're genuinely curious about going further.
  • Language: Fluent in English.
