Title: Marketing Analyst
Description:
Valsea - Where Innovation Meets Entertainment

At Valsea, we’re shaping the future of entertainment with immersive, engaging experiences that captivate players worldwide. Based in sunny Marbella, Spain, our vibrant open-space office is home to an international team of developers, marketers, product owners, and much more.

Valsea operates a leading scalable technology platform, 100% owned by us. This gives us the freedom to deliver results fast. We integrate selected outside companies into our ecosystem to ensure we provide fresh and vibrant products for our customers.

We work in a fast-paced environment, creating new brands, maintaining and improving our tech stack, launching leading acquisition and retention campaigns, and developing fresh features. Our ethos is simple - be best-in-class in everything we do.

Despite our rapid growth, Valsea still has that exciting start-up feel. We embrace a hybrid working model to balance professional growth with personal well-being. With a dynamic work environment, attractive salary, and a culture that thrives on collaboration, our power is our people.

We provide company laptops and other things you may need to work comfortably. Remote workers are always invited to our annual company trip and other team events. Ready to be part of something exciting?

What are we looking for now?

A highly analytical and commercially minded Marketing Analyst to join our BI & Analytics team. In this role, you will be a crucial partner to the Marketing Department, collaborating closely with the CMO and members of the Acquisition team, transforming raw data into the strategic intelligence that drives our business forward.

You will move beyond surface-level reporting to conduct deep, methodologically sound analyses of our marketing campaigns, iGaming products, and player behaviour.

Your primary mission is to challenge assumptions, uncover the "why" behind the numbers, and provide clear, actionable recommendations that optimise our marketing investments, enhance player engagement, and grow our market share.

This is a high-impact role for a data-driven storyteller who is passionate about the iGaming industry and thrives on turning complex questions into profitable business decisions.

What You’ll Do


Design, execute, and deliver post-mortems for marketing and acquisition campaigns, going beyond top-line results to measure true incremental lift, ROI, and profitability, accounting for confounding variables and establishing clear attribution.
Apply a rigorous analytical framework to evaluate the performance of marketing activities, measuring the impact of these "tests" using statistical principles to assess effectiveness and inform future strategy.
Analyse player behaviour by segmenting the user base across various dimensions (e.g., New vs. Existing, Player Value Tiers, Game/Provider Preference) to better understand player loyalty and lifetime value.
Translate complex findings into compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for stakeholders in Marketing and Leadership; these insights will directly influence decisions on marketing spend, product strategy, and promotional calendars.
Develop and maintain powerful, intuitive dashboards and reports in Looker to provide stakeholders with self-serve access to performance metrics.


What You’ll Bring


You will have proven previous experience working as a business data analyst in the domain of online acquisition marketing or a closely related field. Online casino or iGaming industry experience is welcome, but not strictly required.
You hold a bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Marketing or related field.
You have a strong analytical mindset, backed up by relevant skills for the ability to collect, organise, analyse, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy.
You are comfortable working with SQL and writing complex queries to retrieve, combine, transform and model data from different sources.
You have hands-on experience in developing reports and dashboards in data visualisation tools such as Looker, Power BI or similar. Looker and LookML experience is preferred, but not essential.


What We Offer


Hybrid working model (office 3 days/week)
Innovative international company that uses cutting-edge tech equipment
Collaborative teams and agile multicultural environment
Competitive salary with flexible working hours
23 paid vacation days + public holidays
Private health insurance for you and your family
Free Online English/Spanish lessons
Corporate events throughout the year
Ongoing learning and professional development opportunities


In-office Perks Such As


Daily breakfast and freshly cooked lunch 3 days a week
50% discount on personal training sessions and physiotherapy sessions
Games room, full kitchen with hot drinks, fruit, soft drinks
Gym, massage room and much more


At Valsea, we want diversity and inclusion in an environment where unique perspectives and new talents thrive. Join us in creating a workplace where everyone can shine and contribute to our collective success. Protecting your privacy and data security is a longstanding top priority for Valsea. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://valsea.teamtailor.com/data-privacy) to learn more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.

We’re a diverse team with great people from across the world. This position is only available as a hybrid position in Marbella (Spain). Please note that we are unable to sponsor work visas outside the EU.

  • Games room, full kitchen with hot drinks, fruit, soft drinks
  • Gym, massage room and much more

At Valsea, we want diversity and inclusion in an environment where unique perspectives and new talents thrive. Join us in creating a workplace where everyone can shine and contribute to our collective success. Protecting your privacy and data security is a longstanding top priority for Valsea. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://valsea.teamtailor.com/data-privacy) to learn more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.

We’re a diverse team with great people from across the world. This position is only available as a hybrid position in Marbella (Spain). Please note that we are unable to sponsor work visas outside the EU.

Title: Finance BI Analyst
Description:
MTS Globe

MTS Globe is the biggest independently owned incoming company in Europe. Our network includes 66 destination offices in 22 countries in and around the Mediterranean Sea, the Atlantic Ocean, Northern Africa, the Black Sea, the Arabian Gulf and Mexico´s Caribbean Coast.

Since its foundation in 1967, MTS Globe has built up a strong market presence in 27 source markets in Europe and North America. It serves over 10,1 million customers with 31 million overnights per year in over 20.000 hotels.

250 clients have chosen MTS Globe as their preferred destination partner. They enjoy a superior degree of satisfaction because of reliable and high service levels, as well as customized solutions, advanced technological integrations, individual attention to each customer, best quality-price ratios due to large purchasing volumes and synergies based on a strong international network.

1300 employees from 45 nationalities, a strong team of local shareholder partners, combined with central experts for operational, commercial and technological support to ensure a superior value for all MTS Globe clients.

The MTS Globe vision is to continue expanding the network and create new business models in a continuously changing distribution environment. An expansion based on satisfied clients, true partnerships with customers as well as hotel suppliers, and visionary IT solutions.

MTS Globe is an equal opportunities employer.

Descripción de la oferta

We are looking for an enthusiastic individual who wants to grow inside the business intelligence area.

You will join a BI team within the tourism industry with a big focus on data visualization, particularly Microsoft Power BI.

This is a very complete and dynamic role, mixing technical development functions with commercial/business functions, ideal for someone who enjoys multitasking and being involved in different areas of the business.

As a BI Analyst, you will be responsible for:


Develop and maintain high quality reporting solutions to complex problems within the company.
Provide trainings and support to the business community to ensure a good adoption and usage of our tools.
Be in constant contact with the business to understand their analytical needs and translate them into technical requirements.
Collaborating closely with financial teams to support them in their reporting processes and daily operations.
Participate on business trips to meet the teams on the regional offices as needed.
A certificate of disability will be recognised


What we offer?

Join our Wellness programme which promotes both emotional and mental health and offers free legal and financial advice.


Refreshing beverages and a variety of fruits are available for all employees to support a healthy lifestyle.
Advancing your professional growth through language training and technological certifications.
Celebrate with us during our annual events and festive holidays, including Easter, Halloween, Thanksgiving and more.
Join one of our sports communities and practice your favourite sport in great company.
Benefit from special health insurance rates.
Enjoy the convenience of working remotely two days a week, providing a better work-life balance.
We provide access to E-learning platform with courses about a wide range of data engineering tools, subsidized official Microsoft certifications and attendance to training events.


Requisitos


Degree in, Business Administration, Information Technologies, or related careers.
Previous experience in financial or accounting roles.
High level of English, spoken and written.
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Excel.
Good communication skills and comfortable doing presentations.
Customer care oriented and keen on finding solutions to other people’s problems.
Availability to travel (occasionally).


Valued:


Experience in the Tourism industry.
High level of Spanish, spoken and written.
Knowledge of Microsoft Power BI.
Knowledge of T-SQL.
Knowledge of Azure Data tools.

Title: Learning & Performance LMS Analytics Specialist
Description:
Job Summary

IFF is a global leader in flavors, fragrances, food ingredients and health & biosciences. We deliver sustainable innovations that elevate everyday products.

Our People & Culture team is fostering a purpose-driven, inclusive culture where talent thrives, leadership grows, and every employee is empowered to make joy.

Be part of a collaborative, innovative, and data-driven team where your insights will help enhance learning experiences, support talent development, and enable better business decisions across the organization.

Where You'll Make a Difference


Develop, maintain, and continuously enhance dashboards, reports, and visualizations that provide insight into learning participation, completion, engagement, effectiveness, and talent development trends.
Analyze data from multiple sources, including Workday Learning, Human Resources systems, learning technologies, survey tools, and other business platforms.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and recommendations for Human Resources, business leaders, and key stakeholders.
Support the design and implementation of learning measurement and evaluation strategies, including program effectiveness, learner feedback, behavior change indicators, and business outcomes.
Conduct ad hoc analyses to support leadership requests, strategic initiatives, workforce development priorities, and continuous improvement efforts.
Identify opportunities to automate reporting processes, improve data governance practices, and increase operational efficiency.
Support the administration of Workday Learning, including courses, learning programs, campaigns, learning paths, enrollments, assignments, and audiences.
Monitor and maintain learning data quality, ensuring accurate reporting, learner records, completion tracking, and system integrity.
Partner with stakeholders to optimize the learner and administrator experience through process improvements and system enhancements.


What Makes You The Right Fit


Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Analytics, Data Analytics, Information Systems, Learning & Development, Organizational Development, Business Administration, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.
Proven experience in data analytics, business intelligence, reporting, dashboard development, or workforce analytics.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including data modeling, pivot tables, formulas, lookups, charting, and analytical techniques.
Experience building dashboards and visualizations using Power BI or similar business intelligence platforms.
Strong presentation skills, including the ability to develop professional and impactful PowerPoint presentations.
Experience supporting Learning Management Systems, learning operations, Human Resources technologies, or related platforms.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking capabilities with a high level of attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities while working independently and collaboratively across diverse stakeholder groups.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts and data-driven insights to both technical and non-technical audiences.


How Would You Stand Out?


Experience administering Workday Learning or other enterprise Learning Management Systems.
Knowledge of SQL, data automation, or advanced reporting methodologies.
Experience measuring learning effectiveness, talent development outcomes, or employee performance initiatives.


Why Choose Us?


Opportunity to influence learning, talent, and performance strategies through meaningful data insights.
Exposure to global stakeholders and cross-functional projects.
Collaborative and inclusive work environment that values innovation and continuous improvement.
Access to learning and professional development opportunities.
Flexible working arrangements aligned with business needs.
Opportunity to work with modern analytics, reporting, and learning technologies.


We are a global leader in taste, scent, and nutrition, offering our customers a broader range of natural solutions and accelerating our growth strategy. At IFF, we believe that your uniqueness unleashes our potential. We value the diverse mosaic of the ethnicity, national origin, race, age, sex, or veteran status. We strive for an inclusive workplace that allows each of our colleagues to bring their authentic self to work regardless of their religion, gender identity & expression, sexual orientation, or disability.

Visit IFF.com/careers/workplace-diversity-and-inclusion to learn more

Title: Data Analyst – Supply Chain Excellence
Description:
Grupo Aubay precisa incorporar un/a Data Analyst al equipo de Supply Chain Excellence en uno de sus principales clientes. La persona seleccionada participará en el diseño, desarrollo y evolución de productos analíticos en entorno Google Cloud Platform (GCP). En principio sería temporal, pero con posibilidad de continuidad




¿Cuáles van a ser tus funciones?




- Ingesta, análisis y comprensión de datos procedentes de SAP, sistemas legacy y GCP

- Transformación de datos bajo arquitectura Medallion (Bronze, Silver, Gold)

- Diseño y mantenimiento de modelos de datos dimensionales (Kimball)

- Desarrollo de pipelines en Dataform

- Creación de dashboards reutilizables en Looker

- Control y validación de calidad de datos

- Traducción de necesidades de negocio a soluciones analíticas

- Documentación técnica y funcional de modelos y datasets




¿Qué necesitamos que nos aportes?




- 1–2 años de experiencia como Data Analyst o perfil similar

- SQL avanzado

- Experiencia en modelado dimensional (Kimball)

- Conocimientos de Python

- Experiencia o conocimiento en GCP (especialmente BigQuery)

- Nivel alto de inglés (imprescindible)

Tecnologías del entorno

- Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery)

- Dataform

- Looker

- GitHub

- Modelado Kimball




¿Qué podemos ofrecerte?




- Contrato indefinido

- Participación en proyectos de analítica avanzada en Supply Chain

- Trabajo con equipos multidisciplinares internacionales

- Oportunidad de crecimiento en Data & Analytics

- Flexibilidad y modelo híbrido: 2 días de teletrabajo a la semana cuando se tenga conocimiento del proyecto

- Horario: 09:00 a 18:00 horas

- Salario valorable en función de experiencia

Title: Data Quality and AI Readiness Product Analyst
Description:
Location: Barcelona, Spain


Are you passionate about data integrity, governance, and enabling the next generation of AI-driven capabilities? Join Sanofi in one of our corporate functions and play a pivotal role in safeguarding the quality, consistency, and reliability of global Human Capital data. As a Data Quality and AI Readiness Product Analyst within Data Governance & Master Data Management, you will sit at the intersection of data governance, Human Capital technology, and process excellence — driving proactive risk management, resolving global data issues, and ensuring our data is ready to power both operational decisions and AI-driven innovation.

We are an innovative global healthcare company with one purpose: to chase the miracles of science to improve people's lives. We're also a company where you can flourish and grow your career, with countless opportunities to explore, make connections with people, and stretch the limits of what you thought was possible. Ready to get started?

Main Responsibilities


Assess and document the downstream impact of data quality issues across payroll processing, management reporting, third-party integrations, and AI/machine learning model inputs
Conduct structured root cause analyses to distinguish between isolated data errors and systemic issues requiring process or configuration-level intervention
Partner closely with the Global Process Owner (GPO) and Workday Technology teams to define and implement structural fixes, whether through process redesign, system configuration changes, or governance policy updates
Serve as a bridge between data operations and technical teams, translating business data quality requirements into actionable technical specifications
Prepare, validate, and execute data correction actions and remediation loads in compliance with data governance standards and change management protocols
Monitor the ongoing adoption of global HR data standards across regions, business units, and functional teams
Proactively detect and flag the re-introduction of local deviations, non-standard values, or workarounds that undermine global data consistency
Identify and escalate risks to data consistency, AI readiness, and global reporting accuracy at the earliest possible stage
Support organizational cloning and data standardization initiatives through fact-based investigation, evidence gathering, and data profiling
Design, build, and maintain automated data validation routines and anomaly detection scripts to reduce reliance on manual data quality checks
Produce clear, structured investigation outputs following a consistent framework: Issue → Root Cause → Risk → Recommendation


About You


3–5 years of experience in data quality, data governance, HR data management, or a related analytical role
Demonstrated experience working with Workday HCM or comparable enterprise HR platforms (Workday certification or formal training highly valued)
Proven track record of conducting data investigations and delivering structured, actionable findings
Experience working in a global, matrixed organization with cross-functional stakeholders
Proficiency in data analysis tools such as Excel/Power Query, SQL, or equivalent
Experience with Workday reporting, calculated fields, and data auditing capabilities
Familiarity with data remediation processes, including mass data loads and EIB (Enterprise Interface Builder) or equivalent
Understanding of HR data domains: employee records, organizational structures, compensation, payroll inputs, and workforce reporting
Exposure to AI/ML data readiness concepts and understanding of how data quality impacts model performance
Experience with data quality platforms or monitoring tools (e.g., Informatica, Collibra, Ataccama, or similar)
Experience with scripting or automation (e.g., Python, Power Automate, or equivalent) for data validation purposes
Knowledge of GDPR, data privacy regulations, and their implications for HR data management
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to decompose complex data issues and identify root causes with precision
Strong communication skills, with the ability to translate complex data findings into clear narratives for both technical teams and senior leadership
Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build effective working relationships across HR, Finance, Payroll, and IT
Degree in Information Systems, Data Management, Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
YoBrands, part of The Rank Group, is a leading online gaming operator in the Spanish & Portuguese markets with an expanding online and offline portfolio focused on bingo, casino and sports betting with 4 leading brands: Enracha, YoCasino, YoBingo and YoSports

Our goals are to provide entertainment and community to our customers with our websites and apps so that they can access our products from their mobile phones, tablets while communicating with one another.

We offer our own bingo and slots software and more than 2000 games from the best game providers. We also offer live shows such as El Show De YoBingo and YoSports TV!

With offices based in Barcelona, Ceuta & Malta we always strive to bring joy and security to our customers. Additionally, we will be launching soon in other regulated markets!

Job Purpose

Provide insightful, actionable analyses through dashboards, and reports, to impact our revenue, productivity, acquisition, attrition, operational efficiencies, cross-sell strategies, customer behavior, and segmentation.

This role comes with a high rate of exposure to various parts of the business due to regular senior stakeholder engagement.

Main Accountabilities And Responsibilities


Extract, transform and load data from different data sources.
Work with existing and new technologies to shape the development of analytical insight delivery.
Translates customer, transactional and behavioral data into key insights and business deliverables.
Presents findings to key stakeholders, and inputs to negotiations on strategies to drive performance across the business.
Collaborating with team members and stakeholders to build data products that enable others to work with reporting effectively and inform decision-making that solve business problems with data.
Engage with Key stakeholders to develop key metrics to measure business performance.


Knowledge, Expertise And Qualifications


Demonstrated ability to build solutions and user-friendly dashboards and reports using BI tools (PowerBI is a plus).
Experience working with ETL tools (Databricks is a plus).
Experience manipulating data stored in relational databases using SQL (proficiency SQL level required).
Familiar with Agile methodology (JIRA).
Experience engaging with key stakeholders.
Ability to work as part of a team delivering solutions that can be deployed into production.
Ability to take initiative and work independently to manage and deliver end-to-end solutions.
Excellent experience working in a data driven environment.
Ability to gather requirements and map business needs to KPI dashboards.
Creative, innovative thinker with outstanding problem-solving skills.
Meticulous and thorough, with a healthy respect for quality & standards.
Good verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to communicate results to non-technical audiences.
Essential: very good knowledge of English at all levels and native-level Spanish.
Degree qualified.


Personal Qualities


Demonstrate sense of passion and pride about own work.
Positive attitude and adapts well to change.
Foster curiosity which results in new lines of thinking, original insight, and breakthrough ideas for the business.
Encourage and challenge others to investigate and explore alternative solutions and ideas.
Ability to translate and clearly formulate technical issues and solutions to the issues.
Excellent problem solving and analysis skills.
Excellent communication skills- written and verbal.
Excellent presentational skills.
Intervenes if warning signs of problems occur within own area of responsibility.
Forward thinking. Proactive rather than Reactive.
Positive and constructive minded.
Takes responsibility for continuous self –learning.
Attention to detail.


Join us to unlock benefits and opportunities that will boost your career journey in a vibrant, inclusive and fulfilling work environment – so you can #BeYourself

Wellbeing@Rank is important... From hybrid working and colleague support networks to menopause support and weekly PepTalks, we’re here for you.

We’ll also invest in your growth by providing development opportunities, leadership training and cutting-edge industry certifications so you have the tools and resources to help you work, win and grow with us.

Immerse yourself in new cultures and gain international exposure through our global business. Collaborate with colleagues from around the globe.

From pensions to bonus schemes, and private medical insurance to life insurance – we've got you covered.


Our benefits vary by brand and/or location. Please have a chat with your local Talent Acquisition specialist to find out what’s in place in your location.


We are committed to being an inclusive employer, ensuring that we better understand and meet the needs and requirements of our candidates and customers.

We aim to do this by facilitating fair and equal access to our services. If you require a reasonable adjustment to be made, please reach out to let us know ahead of your interview.

  • Creative, innovative thinker with outstanding problem-solving skills.
  • Meticulous and thorough, with a healthy respect for quality & standards.
  • Good verbal and written communication skills.
  • Ability to communicate results to non-technical audiences.
  • Essential: very good knowledge of English at all levels and native-level Spanish.
  • Degree qualified.

Personal Qualities

  • Demonstrate sense of passion and pride about own work.
  • Positive attitude and adapts well to change.
  • Foster curiosity which results in new lines of thinking, original insight, and breakthrough ideas for the business.
  • Encourage and challenge others to investigate and explore alternative solutions and ideas.
  • Ability to translate and clearly formulate technical issues and solutions to the issues.
  • Excellent problem solving and analysis skills.
  • Excellent communication skills- written and verbal.
  • Excellent presentational skills.
  • Intervenes if warning signs of problems occur within own area of responsibility.
  • Forward thinking. Proactive rather than Reactive.
  • Positive and constructive minded.
  • Takes responsibility for continuous self –learning.
  • Attention to detail.

Join us to unlock benefits and opportunities that will boost your career journey in a vibrant, inclusive and fulfilling work environment – so you can #BeYourself

Wellbeing@Rank is important... From hybrid working and colleague support networks to menopause support and weekly PepTalks, we’re here for you.

We’ll also invest in your growth by providing development opportunities, leadership training and cutting-edge industry certifications so you have the tools and resources to help you work, win and grow with us.

Immerse yourself in new cultures and gain international exposure through our global business. Collaborate with colleagues from around the globe.

From pensions to bonus schemes, and private medical insurance to life insurance – we've got you covered.

  • Our benefits vary by brand and/or location. Please have a chat with your local Talent Acquisition specialist to find out what’s in place in your location.

We are committed to being an inclusive employer, ensuring that we better understand and meet the needs and requirements of our candidates and customers.

We aim to do this by facilitating fair and equal access to our services. If you require a reasonable adjustment to be made, please reach out to let us know ahead of your interview.

#YoBrands

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
The Story So Far: We’re Building a Global Brand in Real Estate

Huspy is one of the leading property technology companies in EMEA.Launched in 2020, we now operate in multiple cities across the UAE and Spain, expanding into Saudi Arabia and 3 more European markets by 2026.

Today, we own the largest portion of the UAE mortgage market and are one of the fastest-growing players in every European city we’ve entered.We’ve raised over $140 million (Series A and Series B) from the world’s top investors, including Sequoia Capital, Founders Fund and Balderton Capital, to reshape the homebuying journey through powerful technology and agent-first tools.

We’ve built a SuperApp that empowers real estate agents and mortgage brokers, bringing cutting‑edge technology to one of the world’s most traditional industries. We’re transforming how property transactions happen — faster, smarter, and better for everyone.We’re not slowing down.

The question is: will you be part of what’s next?

The Main Event: What You’ll Drive, Build, and Own


Real Estate Market Modeling: Build models applied to challenges such as valuation/pricing leveraging techniques from classic supervised ML to more advanced approaches.
Multimodal Embeddings: Create vector representations of Real Estate entities, such as listings, combining images, text, and structured attributes to power search, matching, deduping, or recommendations.
Data Analysis & Experimentation: Use SQL/Python to extract, clean, and analyze data; design experiments and evaluate model-product impact with robust metrics.
Model Operationalization: Ship models to production with capabilities such as monitoring, automated rollout, or CI/CD (in partnership with engineering).
Cross-functional Delivery: Partner with product, engineering, and operations teams to translate business problems into scalable ML solutions.


The Perfect Match: What It Takes to Succeed at Huspy


Proven Experience: 4–8 years in applied data science/ML, delivering models that move real-world KPIs.
SQL & Python Mastery: Strong in frameworks such as Pandas/NumPy/Scikit-learn...building reliable data pipelines, model training and evaluation.
MLOps Fundamentals: Experience deploying/maintaining models (batch or real-time), versioning, CI/CD basics, observability, and reproducible training.
Communication & Ownership: Clear with technical/non-technical stakeholders; can scope, prioritize, and explain tradeoffs.
Comfortable with uncertainty, data quality issues, leakage risks, and market dynamics (location, seasonality, inventory shifts).
Nice to Have: Software engineering experience; multimodal/vision experience; voice AI (ASR/NLU) exposure.
Academic Background: Bachelor’s in STEM (Master’s a plus).


By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be collected, processed, and retained by the company solely for the purposes of managing and assessing my candidacy.

Title: Automatization & Reporting Data Analyst
Description:
Proyectos reales, retos desde el primer día

En WSP trabajamos para transformar la ingeniería a través de la innovación, la técnica y la excelencia en el diseño. Nuestros equipos participan en proyectos que combinan complejidad, impacto y ambición. Si acabas de terminar tus estudios o tienes poca experiencia, y quieres crecer rápido en un entorno exigente, éste es tu sitio.

Estamos ampliando nuestro equipo de Bidding & Commercial en Santander o Madrid y buscamos incorporar un perfil con sólidos conocimientos en bases de datos y un marcado interés por el análisis de datos, capaz de ofrecer soporte especializado a nuestros equipos de inteligencia de negocio.

¿Cuál será tu rol?

Buscamos a una persona con sólidos conocimientos en bases de datos y manejo de herramientas de visualización dentro de Power Platform (Power BI, Power Automate y/o Power Apps), capaz de preparar, transformar y estructurar datos empresariales para crear dashboards y automatizar informes y procesos. El rol requiere una alta capacidad de gestión de proyectos de automatización —desde la recogida y análisis de requisitos hasta la planificación, el seguimiento y la evaluación mediante KPIs— trabajando con metodologías ágiles y equipos multidisciplinares. Integrada en el equipo de Data Analysis y Business Intelligence, esta posición tendrá un enfoque de soporte y colaboración con distintos equipos internos para mejorar la calidad, el análisis, la visualización y la consistencia de los datos en la toma de decisiones. Valoramos especialmente un perfil proactivo, con actitud analítica, orientación a resultados y capacidad para desenvolverse en entornos dinámicos.

Entre tus principales funciones se incluyen analizar procesos internos y de clientes para identificar oportunidades de mejora basadas en datos y automatización; diseñar, desarrollar y mantener informes y dashboards en Power BI; crear, optimizar y monitorizar automatizaciones en Power Automate; desarrollar aplicaciones internas o para clientes con Power Apps; gestionar y estructurar espacios de trabajo, listas y bibliotecas; documentar requerimientos técnicos y funcionales; elaborar manuales y guías de usuario; dar soporte en la implantación de nuevas soluciones y ofrecer formación básica; así como colaborar estrechamente con equipos internos para comprender necesidades, priorizar iniciativas y traducirlas en soluciones tecnológicas eficientes y escalables

¿Qué buscamos?

Buscamos un perfil con más de 5 años de experiencia, sólidos conocimientos de SQL y un dominio experto de la plataforma Microsoft Power (Power BI, Power Automate y/o Power Apps), capaz de integrar Power Platform con diversas fuentes de datos y sistemas internos o externos. Valoramos una alta capacidad analítica, organización, atención al detalle y una marcada orientación al aprendizaje continuo, especialmente en tecnologías emergentes que puedan resultar clave para la evolución de la organización.

¿Qué te ofrecemos?


Incorporación a un equipo técnico con proyectos reales desde el primer día
Jornada flexible y plan de formación continua
Acompañamiento de perfiles senior y entorno de aprendizaje real
Retribución flexible y acceso a beneficios para empleados/as


¡Da el primer paso!

Si quieres empezar tu carrera profesional con un equipo que te exija, te apoye y te permita crecer desde el minuto uno, postúlate y construyamos juntos tu trayectoria.

Nuestro Proceso De Selección


Entrevista telefónica: Una primera conversación breve para conocerte mejor y hablar sobre tu experiencia y expectativas.
Entrevista técnica y personal: Te reunirás con nuestro equipo de captación de talento y con el responsable del área para evaluar conjuntamente tus habilidades técnicas y tu encaje en el equipo.
Entrevista final: Conversarás con la Dirección de la Unidad de Negocio sobre tu potencial rol y cómo encaja con nuestra visión estratégica.


Sobre Nosotros

Somos una firma global líder en ingeniería y servicios profesionales. Cuando buscas un cambio, es fundamental elegir un lugar donde puedas crecer profesionalmente y encajar con tus valores. Nuestros equipos están comprometidos con generar un impacto real y duradero en las comunidades en las que trabajamos, impulsando una cultura de excelencia técnica, innovación e inclusión.

Promovemos activamente la diversidad y la igualdad de oportunidades, convencidos de que las diferencias enriquecen nuestro trabajo y fortalecen nuestros resultados. Todas las candidaturas serán tratadas con la máxima confidencialidad y consideración.

Únete al equipo y descubre lo que puedes lograr con nosotros.

#SomosWSP

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About BizAway

Here at BizAway, we Deliver the Future of Travel. 😎

We are a solid international company with strong ambitions and great expertise. With a focus on sustainability 🌱, on a daily basis we support companies enabling them to improve their travel management through our constantly evolving services and solutions, always characterized by our tech attitude and smart and innovative processes. 🚀

We know that success comes from People and deserves to be recognized.👫Proactivity and

Reliability, Kind collaboration, and communication are the core values of our Solution Culture.

If you like challenges and would love to be part of one of the fastest-growing B2B scale-ups, then BizAway is the company you have been looking for. 🤩

We are looking for

We are looking for a driven and enthusiastic Data Analyst (m/w/d) , who desires to contribute to the growth and success of our Data & Analytics Team.

Your role in BizAway


You manage and improve our current reporting system, supporting all internal needs for Data visualization of data findings through charts, graphs, and dashboards to make our data understandable.
You learn all aspects of the travel industry from Finance, Products, Sales and Service/Operations perspective, translating data into actionable insights resulting in verifiable reporting.
By having domain expertise in our industry, you engage with Business and Strategy stakeholders to help define KPIs, including how data will be captured, shared, and leveraged across our departments.
You contribute to creating the base foundations of a comprehensive source of truth through data modeling actions


Your Basic Qualifications


Previously worked in a Tech Company
Demonstrated experience with Product Analytics tools (eg: Amplitude, Mixpanel, Pendo, Google Analytics)
Demonstrated experience with BI Data Visualization tools (Qlik preferred)
Data analysis, presentation, and visualization experience
Strong communication skills required
Ability to work alone or in a team as required
Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Engineering, Economics or a related field is preferred
English proficiency (written and spoken level B2)


We are also interested in


Knowledge of programming languages such as Python
Experience with ETL tools
Understanding of AI/ML concepts


Our offer

📄 Contract type: Permanent

🕓 Working hours: Full-time – 40h/week, from Monday to Friday

🏢 Hybrid setting, flexible working policy, and great offices in Europe

💰 Salary: Attractive compensation, including equity in the company

💼 Equity in the company (stock options plan)

✈️ A seat on a scale-up with skyrocketing growth

📈 Development of your entrepreneurial spirit, having the chance to implement real-impact business decision

🌍Multicultural and international team

🙌Collaborative and smart environment to work and learn

🍻Free coffee to kick-start your day and free beers to celebrate together

BizAway avoids any discrimination based on age, gender, sexual orientation, health status, nationality, political opinions, and religious beliefs in all decisions affecting personnel selection.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
En Kids&Us diseñamos experiencias que conectan con la emoción de aprender inglés.

Combinamos pedagogía, tecnología y datos para mejorar continuamente la experiencia de aprendizaje de miles de niños/as y familias en todo el mundo. A través del dato, impulsamos decisiones más inteligentes y construimos las bases que nos permiten seguir creciendo con impacto.

En este contexto de evolución, buscamos un/a Data Analyst que analice y explote los datos para generar insights, reportings y cuadros de mando que apoyen la toma de decisiones y la evolución del negocio.

¿Por qué te encantará formar parte de Kids&Us?


Un producto con propósito real.
Espacio para construir y dejar huella.
Un equipo cercano, motivado y multidisciplinar que combina aprendizaje, diseño y tecnología. Nos gusta compartir logros, aprender y celebrar el camino: organizamos team buildings, town halls, eventos especiales y… ¡mucho más! 😊
Modalidad de trabajo híbrida (2 días en HQ y 3 de teletrabajo).
Horario flexible de lunes a jueves, y jornada intensiva los viernes y durante el verano.
Día libre en tu cumpleaños, descuentos para tu familia y más beneficios pensados para cuidarte y crecer profesionalmente.


Tu rol


Analizar datos y generar insights accionables para las distintas áreas de negocio (producto, operaciones, corporate).
Traducir necesidades de negocio en soluciones analíticas, definiendo KPIs y métricas que permitan medir el rendimiento.
Diseñar y mantener dashboards e informes, asegurando una visualización clara, útil y orientada a la toma de decisiones.
Dar soporte a equipos de negocio en sus necesidades de reporting y análisis, facilitando el acceso y uso del dato.
Velar por la calidad y consistencia de los datos utilizados en los análisis y cuadros de mando.
Colaborar estrechamente con el equipo de Data en la validación de modelos de datos y en la evolución del ecosistema de analytics.
Participar en la evolución del servicio de analytics y en futuras iniciativas de advanced analytics o data science.


Así brillarás en este puesto


Grado en Estadística, Matemáticas, Ingeniería, ADE o similar, y valorable máster o especialización en Data Analytics o Business Intelligence.
Entre 2 y 4 años de experiencia en roles de Data Analyst o similares, trabajando en análisis de datos y reporting.
Experiencia sólida en análisis de datos, SQL y herramientas de BI (power BI, Tableau, etc.) y de product analytics (Amplitude o similares).
Persona analítica, con capacidad de síntesis, orientación a negocio y buenas habilidades de comunicación.
Nivel de inglés avanzado.


Ready to inspire?

Si te motiva trabajar con datos, entender el negocio y generar impacto real a través del análisis y los insights, este es tu reto. ¡Queremos conocerte!

Kids&Us embraces diversity and is committed to attracting qualified candidates who also embrace and value diversity and inclusivity. Kids&Us is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We stand against discimination or harassment of any kind. We embrace the uniqueness in everyone and we encourage each individual to be their true selves.

Información legal

Cláusula informativa: Responsable del tratamiento: Kids&Us English, S.L. con domicilio en avenida Tudela, 12, 08242 Manresa (Barcelona). Finalidad: gestión de CV, perfiles y candidaturas profesionales. Destinatarios: Kids&Us English, S.L. y toda su red de centros y franquicias adscritos. Derechos: tiene derecho de acceso, rectificación, supresión, oposición, limitación del tratamiento, portabilidad de los datos, a no estar sometido a decisiones individuales automatizadas y a la revocación. Para ejercer los anteriores derechos, así como para cualquier consulta relacionada con el tratamiento de sus datos, puede dirigirse al responsable del tratamiento al correo electrónico info@kidsandus.com. Tiene más información en la página https://www.kidsandus.es/es/footer/politica-de-privacidad

  • Persona analítica, con capacidad de síntesis, orientación a negocio y buenas habilidades de comunicación.
  • Nivel de inglés avanzado.

Ready to inspire?

Si te motiva trabajar con datos, entender el negocio y generar impacto real a través del análisis y los insights, este es tu reto. ¡Queremos conocerte!

Kids&Us embraces diversity and is committed to attracting qualified candidates who also embrace and value diversity and inclusivity. Kids&Us is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We stand against discimination or harassment of any kind. We embrace the uniqueness in everyone and we encourage each individual to be their true selves.

Title: Clinical Data Analyst
Description:
About Analog Devices

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).

Clinical Data Analyst

Essential Duties And Responsibilities


Analyze and manage large datasets from diverse sources—including deployed products, clinical studies, patients EMR/EHR, and internal R&D—to support the development of chronic disease management solutions.
Design and perform rigorous statistical analyses to generate solid insights to support clinical efficacy and benefits of the product
Produce product quality insights that support quality assurance activities in accordance with medical quality management system procedures.
Construct data pipelines and engage data cleaning and organization by integrating multiple data streams in collaboration with cross-functional teams to enable efficient analysis at scale.
Communicate findings effectively to stakeholders through clear, actionable reports and presentations.


Minimum Qualifications


Master’s degree in Statistics, Data Science, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science with biomedical application research, Biomedical Engineering or a related field.
Strong foundation in data science, statistical methodologies, and hypothesis test design.
Basic understanding of human systems physiology.
Familiarity with wearable sensor signals, including bio-potential measurements (e.g., ECG) and audio signals.
Proficiency in programming languages, basic engineering tools, and statistical tools (e.g., Python, MATLAB, R, and etc.).
Excellent communication and presentation skills for engaging both external medical professionals and internal multidisciplinary teams.


Additional Desirable Skills, Knowledges And Abilities


Experience in publishing clinical journal articles is a plus
Experience collaborating with medical professionals / clinicians.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic work environment.


For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.

Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.

Job Req Type: Experienced

Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time

Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days

Title: Product Data Analyst | EU based/Remote
Description:
Join Hostinger, and we’ll grow fast! 🚀

We’re shaping the future of online success - powered by AI and driven by people. With 900+ talented professionals and over 5 million clients in 150 countries, we help creators and entrepreneurs bring their ideas to life faster and easier than ever before.

Our mission: To provide tools that help individuals and small businesses succeed online faster and easier.

Our culture: Guided by 10 company principles.

Our formula for success: Customer obsession, innovative products, and talented teams.

Your day-to-day:


Partner with Product teams to tackle challenges, reveal untapped potential, challenge status-quo and empower data-driven choices.
Use Amplitude to analyse user journeys and conduct A/B tests.
Use SQL and Tableau to extract and analyze data.
Build dashboards that make an impact - create powerful visualizations that give teams at Hostinger the visibility and confidence to take action, while ensuring data integrity and validity.
Use Lumos - our internal AI data agent to surface new insights and business recommendations.
Collaborate with our top-notch Data Platform team - work closely with data engineers and analytics engineers to ensure data availability, efficient processes and successful project execution.


Real projects you might work on:


Analysing AI-Powered Marketing Tool Users - our AI-powered Reach Team needs to understand product usage, user segments and conversions. Your insights could help us refine our approach and shape the future of Hostinger's Reach product. If you want to go the extra mile - some product-related user flow analysis and A/B testing would be welcome too!
Decoding AI WebMail Clients Engagement - our Mail Team is rapidly evolving the communication experience, and they require deep analytical support to fuel their growth. You will dive into customer behavior patterns, providing the strategic clarity needed to shape the next generation of Hostinger’s email solutions through data-backed innovation.
Optimizing Our Client Referral Program - As this initiative scales rapidly, your data-driven expertise will be essential in refining our commission frameworks. You will help us architect high-impact partnership strategies, ensuring we build deeper, more rewarding connections with our global client base.
Empowering Our Innovation Team - Innovation is at the core of our success. As we explore diverse new ventures, your analytical expertise will be vital in enriching these ideas with data. You will provide strategic guidance to Product Managers on whether and how to scale emerging business opportunities effectively.
Defining Product Adoption Success - We want to ensure our clients navigate our tools seamlessly and reach their objectives with ease. You will dive deep into product usage patterns and customer behavior, providing strategic recommendations to elevate the overall Hostinger experience through data-driven improvements.
And many more, depending on your skills, interests and business needs.


Your skills and experience:


2+ years of experience in a product analyst, data analyst, business analyst or similar role.
Experience with A/B testing and user journey analysis.
Hands-on SQL expertise - you can write queries, create and optimize dashboard models. Basically, Claude asks you to check queries.
Proficiency in data visualization tools - we use Tableau, but experience with other tools works too.
A results-driven mindset - you love turning raw data into clear, actionable insights that drive business impact.
Strong communication & collaboration skills - you can explain complex data in a simple way and work well with cross-functional teams.
Bonus: Experience with Amplitude or other product analytics tools.
Extra points for: experience with Python or R for data analysis, A/B testing experience or knowledge of statistical concepts.


Benefits for you:


🚀 360 Growth: We provide limitless learning opportunities: access to platforms like Reforge and CoachHub, global conferences, feedback culture, digital libraries, and industry-leading subscriptions. Advance your career with internal mobility, and grow with a team eager to share knowledge and support your success.
🎯 Freedom & responsibility: Work on your terms, whether from the comfort of home or anywhere in the world. With a home office budget, you can elevate your workspace. Enjoy the flexibility to manage your schedule while bringing your ideas to life in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
💪 Wellness simplified: Your health comes first with company-provided sick days, 20 paid suspension days, Headspace subscriptions, and recharge leave. Participate in wellbeing-dedicated events and simply enjoy the balance of a lifestyle that prioritizes wellness.
🎉 Work hard - party hard: Recognize hard work with company events like Town Hall, Meet the Client initiatives, team-building experiences, and workations. Celebrate life’s big moments with milestone gifts for weddings, new parenthood, and graduations.


Compensation:

We’re looking for people who can make a meaningful impact, and we recognize that different levels of experience bring different levels of value. Compensation for this role will be discussed throughout the hiring process and tailored based on your skills, experience, and potential contribution to the team.

Get ready to take your personal and professional growth to new heights! Join Hostinger today and be part of our journey 🚀

Three. Two. Onboard

Title: DATA ANALYST
Description:
Our Essence

Patria is, essentially, a long-lasting and resilient entrepreneurial Partnership, that continually assesses the environment and identifies trends. We quickly adapt our investments and product offering to capture investment opportunities derived from such analysis, delivering consistent results to our clients and creating long-lasting impact for all our stakeholders. We are home to professionals who carry this entrepreneurial spirit as a live and dynamic Partnership, where new members are welcome, and we evolve with their expertise and cultural characteristics. We know how to transform ideas into real business opportunities and are engaged in the development and perpetuation of the Partnership.

About Patria

Patria is made of high performers like you. Behind every result we achieve as a team, there are talented people with unique set of skills: From an entrepreneurial spirit to high a performance mindset. From high-level expertise to a hands-on approach.

It is through these qualities that we have fostered trusting relationships with each of our clients. We proudly highlight our high returns to our investors coupled with our unwavering commitment to ethical values.

That’s why we have been known as the regional and sector specialist in alternative asset management with on-the-ground expertise everywhere we operate. Patria anticipates trends to create long-lasting impact for all our stakeholders for over 35 years. We envision to keep delivering consistent results to better serve our clients.

Purpose of the position

To manage and ensure the optimal functioning of the databases, guaranteeing their availability, integrity, security, and reliability of information, in order to support business operations and decision-making.

Functions And Responsibilities


Create, design, manage, and monitor databases, ensuring their proper functioning.
Guarantee the availability, integrity, security, and reliability of information.
Optimize performance through continuous adjustments and maintenance.
Address incidents and resolve database-related problems.
Document technical procedures and configurations.
Design, build, and manage complex dashboards integrating multiple data sources.
Propose and implement improvements to data analysis, visualization, and consumption processes.
Monitor and ensure compliance with regulatory limits for managed vehicles.
Generate reports for internal and external use.


Required Profile


Academic Background: Bachelor's degree in Systems Engineering, Software Engineering, or related field.
Language: English B1 level.
Experience: Minimum 2 years in database administration.
Technical Skills: Familiarity with office tools such as Access, SQL, Python, and Power BI.


Diversity

At Patria we do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class. Our employment policies strictly prohibit all forms of discrimination on any of the grounds in relevant laws. All our employment practices are solely based on individual merit and our organization's needs at the time.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Scopely is seeking a highly motivated Analytics Engineer to join our Playgami team in Barcelona on a hybrid basis!

At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day - whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers who are developing, publishing, and innovating the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people worldwide daily.

Playgami is building the best SaaS gaming platform ever! The team strives to create the world’s best products for game production, operation, and insights. Powering many award-winning, loved-by-millions franchise games over mobile, web, and PC.

What You’ll Do


We collaborate with engineers, product teams, and analysts to create self-serve insight products that are fast, accurate, and elastic at a petabyte-scale
We work across the full data product lifecycle: designing new tracking plans, developing data models, ELT pipelines, and self-serve insight products like Looker explores and dashboards
We are stewards of data quality, ensuring that metrics are consistently reported across the catalog of data products
We work with cutting-edge tech (BigQuery, dbt, Looker)
We look forward and back, applying tools like BQML to predict player behavior
We empathize with our game product teams and departments to make their daily work more enjoyable and their products more successful
We often specialize in some part of the data stack and lifecycle but always aim to contribute across the full spectrum


What We’re Looking For


You love solving puzzles
You can translate needs into analytics tech
You have experience with the modern data stack (tools like BigQuery, Snowflake, dbt, Looker, Airflow, GitHub, and command line-based workflows) or hunger to work in that domain
You have first-class SQL skills and 3+ years of experience in a relevant role
You understand the importance of data modeling, like knowing your Kimballs, your star schemas
You value business context and improvement as much as you do first-class technical solutions
You love collaboration and take pride in working in high-functioning teams
You enjoy working in rapidly evolving, hyper-growth environments where every change presents an opportunity for us to grow
You love having fun at work. Ping Pong/Mario Kart skills are a plus!


At Scopely, we create games for everyone - and want to ensure that the people behind our games reflect that! We are committed to creating a diverse, supportive work environment where everyone is treated with respect. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us & embrace the adventure!

About Scopely

Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live-operates one of the most diversified and award-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play.

Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the #1 mobile games company in the U.S. and #2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Scopely Explore games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come.

Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play.

Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/.

Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com.

Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice.

Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

Title: Data Analyst – Power BI
Description:
🚀 Estamos buscando talento en Data & Business Intelligence

Seguimos creciendo y queremos reforzar nuestro equipo de IT con un/a especialista en Power BI y análisis de datos que nos ayude a llevar nuestra toma de decisiones al siguiente nivel.

📊 ¿Qué harás en este rol?

Serás una pieza clave en el desarrollo y evolución de nuestro entorno de Business Intelligence:

Diseñar y mantener dashboards e informes en Power BI.
Crear y optimizar modelos de datos.
Desarrollar métricas y cálculos avanzados con DAX.
Trabajar procesos ETL con Power Query.
Integrar datos de distintas fuentes (ERP, e-commerce, etc.).
Colaborar con diferentes áreas para entender necesidades de negocio.
Asegurar la calidad, fiabilidad y rendimiento de los datos.
Aplicar buenas prácticas de gobierno del dato.

🎯 ¿Qué buscamos?

Experiencia sólida en Power BI.
Nivel avanzado en DAX, Power Query y modelado de datos.
Conocimientos de SQL.
Experiencia trabajando con grandes volúmenes de datos.
Perfil analítico, orientado a negocio y con autonomía.
Capacidad para interactuar con diferentes equipos.

💡 Muy valorable

Conocimientos en Microsoft Fabric, Azure, Python, Power Apps o Power Automate.

✨ ¿Qué te ofrecemos?

Proyecto en crecimiento con alto impacto en la compañía.
Participación directa en la evolución de la estrategia de datos.
Entorno dinámico, colaborativo y con retos constantes.

Si te apasionan los datos y quieres formar parte de un proyecto con recorrido, ¡nos encantará conocerte!

Title: Financial Data Analyst
Description:
Ubicación:

A Coruña, ES, 15001

Fecha de publicación: 28 jun 2026

¿Te defines por tu talento y tu visión?

Entonces, tú también puedes marcar la diferencia. Únete a un entorno profesional que contribuye a la transformación de empresas y sociedad. Alcanza tus metas, supera tus límites y únete a una firma que va más allá de los servicios profesionales.

Porque marcar la diferencia no es solo algo que decimos. Es lo que hacemos.

Desarrolla tu carrera con nosotros.

En KPMG K-TIA (Tecnología e Innovación en Auditoría) contamos con un equipo excepcional, una amplia gama de servicios, recursos tecnológicos de vanguardia, una potente red global y una estrategia clara para llegar al mercado. Por ello, nuestro equipo de Auditoría IT está en proceso de expansión y buscamos incorporar nuevo talento.

Actualmente, estamos buscando dos analistas de datos senior para integrarse en nuestro departamento en la oficina de A Coruña.

¿Qué podrás hacer?


Participar en pruebas de D&A dentro de los trabajos de auditoría, contribuyendo a entregar resultados de calidad en los plazos establecidos.
Apoyar en el desarrollo de soluciones en Power BI según las necesidades del equipo y del cliente.
Colaboración en proyectos de integración con clientes mediante la extracción y el modelado de datos; ejecución de pruebas en los ámbitos SCIIF y SCIIS; realizando de pruebas de auditoría; la revisión y elaboración de KPIs; y preparación de conclusiones e informes finales.


¿Qué necesitas saber?


Formación: Grado en Ingeniería, ADE o similar (valorable especialización en análisis de datos).
Experiencia: Más de 3 años en funciones relacionadas con análisis de datos contables.
Conocimientos técnicos: Excel avanzado, SQL básico, Power BI (nivel intermedio-avanzado). Valorando positivamente conocimientos adicionales en Azure Databricks o Snowflake, y lenguajes de programación Python, R y Scala o Java.
Idiomas: Nivel de inglés alto.
Se valorará experiencia previa en auditoría IT, autonomía, iniciativa y capacidad para identificar necesidades y proponer soluciones.


¿Qué valor añadido te podemos aportar?


Un gran ambiente de trabajo, tanto dentro como fuera de la oficina
Oportunidades internacionales y red de contactos global
Formacióncontinuay plan de carrera a tu medida
Salario competitivo y plan de remuneración flexible
31 días laborables de vacaciones
La tarde de tu cumpleaños libre
Flexibilidad y posibilidad de teletrabajo
Acceso a Kteam, nuestra plataforma de bienestar, servicios, solidaridad y promociones
Los beneficios pueden variar para programas de becas y/o prácticas.


Nuestro compromiso en KPMG es promover ambientes de trabajo en los que se trate con respeto y dignidad a las personas, garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades en su selección, formación y promoción ofreciendo un entorno de trabajo libre de cualquier discriminación por motivo de género, edad, discapacidad, orientación sexual, identidad o expresión de género, religión, etnia, estado civil o cualquier otra circunstancia personal o social. Y es que cada persona tiene un valor único y especial que aportar a la firma.

Nuestros valores marcan la diferencia. Marca la diferencia, impulsa tu talento.

Title: Data Analyst (BigQuery, Looker, SQL)
Description:
En Grupo Aubay buscamos un/a Analista de Datos con buen nivel de SQL para participar en todo el ciclo analítico: obtención de datos desde SAP y otras fuentes, transformación en BigQuery, modelado para reporting y creación de dashboards en Looker.




La persona seleccionada tendrá un rol muy orientado a negocio dentro del área de Supply Chain y con exposición a tecnologías modernas como Google Cloud Platform.




¿Cuáles serán tus principales funciones?

Ingesta, análisis y comprensión de datos procedentes de SAP, sistemas legacy y GCP
Transformación de datos bajo arquitectura Medallion (Bronze, Silver, Gold)
Diseño y mantenimiento de modelos de datos dimensionales (Kimball)
Desarrollo de pipelines en Dataform
Creación de dashboards reutilizables en Looker
Control y validación de calidad de datos
Traducción de necesidades de negocio a soluciones analíticas
Documentación técnica y funcional de modelos y datasets




Requisitos

1–2 años de experiencia como Data Analyst o perfil similar
SQL avanzado
Experiencia en modelado dimensional (Kimball)
Conocimientos de Python
Experiencia o conocimiento en GCP (especialmente BigQuery)
Nivel alto de inglés (imprescindible)
Tecnologías del entorno: GCP (BigQuery), Dataform, Looker, GitHub, Modelado Kimball




Se ofrece

Participación en proyectos de analítica avanzada en Supply Chain
Horario de 9h a 18h
Modelo híbrido de trabajo
Trabajo con equipos multidisciplinares internacionales
Retribución flexible

Title: Product Data Analyst | EU based/Remote
Description:
Join Hostinger, and we’ll grow fast! 🚀

We’re shaping the future of online success - powered by AI and driven by people. With 900+ talented professionals and over 5 million clients in 150 countries, we help creators and entrepreneurs bring their ideas to life faster and easier than ever before.

Our mission: To provide tools that help individuals and small businesses succeed online faster and easier.

Our culture: Guided by 10 company principles.

Our formula for success: Customer obsession, innovative products, and talented teams.

Your day-to-day:


Partner with Product teams to tackle challenges, reveal untapped potential, challenge status-quo and empower data-driven choices.
Use Amplitude to analyse user journeys and conduct A/B tests.
Use SQL and Tableau to extract and analyze data.
Build dashboards that make an impact - create powerful visualizations that give teams at Hostinger the visibility and confidence to take action, while ensuring data integrity and validity.
Use Lumos - our internal AI data agent to surface new insights and business recommendations.
Collaborate with our top-notch Data Platform team - work closely with data engineers and analytics engineers to ensure data availability, efficient processes and successful project execution.


Real projects you might work on:


Analysing AI-Powered Marketing Tool Users - our AI-powered Reach Team needs to understand product usage, user segments and conversions. Your insights could help us refine our approach and shape the future of Hostinger's Reach product. If you want to go the extra mile - some product-related user flow analysis and A/B testing would be welcome too!
Decoding AI WebMail Clients Engagement - our Mail Team is rapidly evolving the communication experience, and they require deep analytical support to fuel their growth. You will dive into customer behavior patterns, providing the strategic clarity needed to shape the next generation of Hostinger’s email solutions through data-backed innovation.
Optimizing Our Client Referral Program - As this initiative scales rapidly, your data-driven expertise will be essential in refining our commission frameworks. You will help us architect high-impact partnership strategies, ensuring we build deeper, more rewarding connections with our global client base.
Empowering Our Innovation Team - Innovation is at the core of our success. As we explore diverse new ventures, your analytical expertise will be vital in enriching these ideas with data. You will provide strategic guidance to Product Managers on whether and how to scale emerging business opportunities effectively.
Defining Product Adoption Success - We want to ensure our clients navigate our tools seamlessly and reach their objectives with ease. You will dive deep into product usage patterns and customer behavior, providing strategic recommendations to elevate the overall Hostinger experience through data-driven improvements.
And many more, depending on your skills, interests and business needs.


Your skills and experience:


2+ years of experience in a product analyst, data analyst, business analyst or similar role.
Experience with A/B testing and user journey analysis.
Hands-on SQL expertise - you can write queries, create and optimize dashboard models. Basically, Claude asks you to check queries.
Proficiency in data visualization tools - we use Tableau, but experience with other tools works too.
A results-driven mindset - you love turning raw data into clear, actionable insights that drive business impact.
Strong communication & collaboration skills - you can explain complex data in a simple way and work well with cross-functional teams.
Bonus: Experience with Amplitude or other product analytics tools.
Extra points for: experience with Python or R for data analysis, A/B testing experience or knowledge of statistical concepts.


Benefits for you:


🚀 360 Growth: We provide limitless learning opportunities: access to platforms like Reforge and CoachHub, global conferences, feedback culture, digital libraries, and industry-leading subscriptions. Advance your career with internal mobility, and grow with a team eager to share knowledge and support your success.
🎯 Freedom & responsibility: Work on your terms, whether from the comfort of home or anywhere in the world. With a home office budget, you can elevate your workspace. Enjoy the flexibility to manage your schedule while bringing your ideas to life in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
💪 Wellness simplified: Your health comes first with company-provided sick days, 20 paid suspension days, Headspace subscriptions, and recharge leave. Participate in wellbeing-dedicated events and simply enjoy the balance of a lifestyle that prioritizes wellness.
🎉 Work hard - party hard: Recognize hard work with company events like Town Hall, Meet the Client initiatives, team-building experiences, and workations. Celebrate life’s big moments with milestone gifts for weddings, new parenthood, and graduations.


Compensation:

We’re looking for people who can make a meaningful impact, and we recognize that different levels of experience bring different levels of value. Compensation for this role will be discussed throughout the hiring process and tailored based on your skills, experience, and potential contribution to the team.

Get ready to take your personal and professional growth to new heights! Join Hostinger today and be part of our journey 🚀

Three. Two. Onboard

Title: Masterdata & Pricing Specialist
Description:
At Nexans, our people and performance drive our industrial success. Folllowing the acquisition of Cables RCT in 2025, Nexans Iberia is entering an exciting transformation phase, integrating two Spanish entities into Nexans Goup standards, governance and systems.

To support this journey, we are recruiting a Masterdata & Pricing Analyst to join Iberia.

The Masterdata and Pricing Analyst works at the junction between Marketing, Sales and Finance. His/her daily role is to provide data intelligence by gathering, consolidating and analyzing partner/customer and pricing data in order to optimize pricing of Nexans offers and to improve profitability results.

To perform this role, he/she can rely on strong data analytics capabilities and works in cooperation with other functions by providing analysis and insights.

The Masterdata & Pricing Analyst works on the following areas:

Main business expectations:


Consolidate and analyze partner/customer and market data
Ensure the quality of the data in the BU
Identify discrepancy causes and provide insights for pricing strategy
Define solutions to upgrade data management tools and steer its deployment to Sales & Marketing functions



Other business expectations:


Participate to upgrade pricing policy
Measure evaluation and recommend adjustments



Key dependencies for maximizing collaboration and subsidiarity


Collaborate with Sales and Marketing teams
Work hand in hand with Market Intelligence & Pricing Manager
Coordinate with Demand Planner



Required Skills And Qualifications


3+ years experience gathering, consolidating and analyzing partner/customer pricing data.
Industrial company experience strongly preferred.
Fluent English and Spanish.
Ability to operate both strategically and hands-on.
Key competencies: collaboration & teamwork, analytical skills, customer, Product and Pricing data monitoring, analysis.

Title: Data & Yield Analyst
Description:
About Softonic

At Softonic, people come first. We’re a multicultural team passionate about building digital experiences used by millions of users worldwide. Guided by a fail fast, learn faster mindset, we encourage curiosity, experimentation, and continuous improvement, to make a real impact.

Our culture is shaped by four principles:


Step up: Take ownership and act with integrity.
Work smart: Focus on impact and efficiency.
One team: Collaborate openly and support each other.
Think big: Stay curious and challenge the status quo.


Team up with us to grow your career in an international, collaborative, and people-focused company where your ideas matter and your work reaches a global audience.

About The Role

We are a global premium publisher with 28 years of history, providing quality software & APPs download services across multiple languages, also expanding with white labels (Download.com Filehippo), Publisher network (rev.amp), and more partnerships on the way.

As our data & yield analyst reporting to the Director of Advertising, you will be responsible for bridging all revenue team’s data-analytics need (including but not limited to datasets, data visualization, and automation with the process for revenue generation/optimization purposes) with our BI team, to drive initiatives that will boost making more & better data-driven decisions and improve efficiency on revenue generation. Will also take the yield function to analyze market trends & discover business opportunities, not only providing insights to the relevant teams (Sales, Paid, Partnership, Adtech, Product, Traffic, etc) but also following through with the implementation of the initiatives to ensure the business impact is realized.

Key Responsibilities


Be a data champion inside the Advertising & RevOps team and the go-to-person for ithe team & stakeholders from other revenue teams when it comes to better utilization of our data.
Help revenue teams to identify opportunities: using new data points, visualization and automation solutions and ideas to improve revenue generation efficiency, take those requirements, coordinate with dev (BI or other teams) to build the roadmap for the projects & implement accordingly.
Oversee & analyze mid-long (monthly, quarterly, YoY) term yield performance/trends of revenue streams & devices, provide insights on fluctuation, alerts when needed, and recommend a clear plan of action.
Provide A/B test or lean test analysis with recommendations for revenue-related initiatives.
Manage UPR optimisation and automation of pricing recommendation tool (either 3rd party or in-house).
Be able to use agentic AI tools to automate process & streamline data processing & analytic flows to deliver results efficiently
Provide insights on market trends, opportunities, and inventory availability information to the sales & AMs team for better usage of market data
Help teams turn data into actionable insights that unlock revenue opportunities and support smarter business decisions.


Must have


At least 2 years of working experience working as business analyst, preferably with experience in digital advertising business.
Experience of working with BI platforms, like Tableau, Qlikview & advance skills of Google Analytics.
Experience of doing data analysis work with AI tools like Claude, Gemini, OpenAI, Copilot, etc.
Experience of working with BigQuery, SQL & Python.
