Solid knowledge of Data Warehousing concepts, data modeling, and relational databases.
Experience building reports, dashboards, and data visualizations using BI tools.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and data interpretation skills.
Experience working with large and complex datasets.
Knowledge of data quality, data validation, and reporting best practices.
Ability to gather business requirements and translate them into technical data solutions.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with experience working directly with business users and technical teams.
Good level of english.




Nice to Have

Experience within Global Markets, Capital Markets, Investment Banking, or Financial Services.

Experience working in Agile environments.

Title: Analista de dados
Description:
Mutual Respect, Empathy, Innovation, and Professional Pride. Do you see yourself within these values?




Founded in 2006 with the mission "To Challenge Complexity", Closer Consulting is a Data Science company specializing in Business Intelligence, Advanced Analytics, and Artificial Intelligence.

Developing projects in the most diverse industries – Banking, Telecommunications, Healthcare, Retail, Energy, Insurance, and Aviation, among others –, we have created an environment where the brightest enquiring minds feel at home, providing our people with the means, opportunities and support they need to thrive and innovate.




We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst to take on new challenges in a hybrid work model in Porto.




Are you the one?




What You'll Do

Process Automation: Analyse, re-engineer, and automate complex risk management processes using Python, adhering to production-level coding standards.
Tool & App Development: Build interactive visualization tools, bots, and APIs to streamline risk decision-making and operational efficiency.
Cross-Margining Deliverables: Collaborate closely with risk managers to develop specialized tools for cross-margining and dynamic risk monitoring.
Production-Ready Coding: Work hand-in-hand with RISK IT to write clean, maintainable code directly within official production environments.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Translate complex user requirements from risk analysts into practical, high-impact digital solutions.

Required Skills

Education: Master’s degree in Finance, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Experience: 2 to 5 years of professional experience in financial markets, risk management, or quantitative data analysis.
Financial Domain Knowledge: Strong understanding of risk management concepts and familiarity with cross-margining.
Python Mastery: Solid working knowledge of Python focused on data analysis, with hands-on experience in Pandas, NumPy, and building APIs.
Engineering Best Practices: Familiarity with full-stack concepts (e.g. HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and modern software development practices.
Data & Analytics: Exceptional ability to process, simplify, and extract insights from large, structured datasets.
Communication: Expert level in English, with the ability to articulate complex concepts clearly to stakeholders and technical teams.




If you believe in this match as much as we do, send us your CV to lia.giannone@closer.pt and reach the closer side of life.




Closer is a company that values diversity and inclusion. We are committed to fostering an environment where everyone is treated with dignity and respect, regardless of race, gender, beliefs, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, or other personal characteristics.




At Closer, we embrace the unique perspectives and experiences that everyone brings, and we believe that it is a fundamental driver for the success we've achieved.

Title: BI/Data Business Analyst (Hibrido, Porto)
Description:
Join our team and help shape the Business Intelligence and Regulatory/Compliance Reporting landscape within a large, regulated financial services organization.

You'll work in a modern data environment featuring a three-layer relational architecture (ODS, Data Warehouse, and Data Marts), daily ETL processes built with Informatica and Python, and Power BI reporting powered by tabular and semantic models.

If you're passionate about data, analytics, and delivering high-quality reporting solutions in a regulated environment, we'd love to hear from you.

Principais Responsabilidades


Gather and clarify business requirements across technical and functional stakeholders.
Produce functional and technical specifications, test plans, workshop reports and supporting documentation.
Support project delivery and evolutionary/corrective maintenance from requirements through production deployment.
Coordinate testing and UAT activities and contribute to application integration and user support.
Extract, manipulate and analyse data, structure working groups and facilitate cross-functional collaboration.


Requisitos mínimos


Strong Oracle SQL skills.
Power BI experience, particularly semantic model design and construction.
Working knowledge of SSAS and dataflows.
Strong communication, organisation, planning and stakeholder-management skills.
Professional written and spoken English, including reporting and documentation.
Experience with Agile delivery and collaboration tools such as Scrum/Kanban, Jira and Confluence.

Title: Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Place: Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto, Portugal
Description:
A NIW é uma empresa de Consultoria que atua em diversas áreas, com destaque para as Tecnologias de Informação. Fazemos parte da Salvador Caetano e a nossa missão é acompanhar, apoiar e assegurar os diversos negócios do Grupo.




Sabias que o setor automóvel é um dos setores de maior inovação e de reinvenção na atualidade? A tecnologia e a digitalização são fatores chave para a sua evolução.




Se estás à procura de desafios em diversas áreas, não gostas de rotina e queres evoluir, este é o lugar certo para ti! Contamos contigo para juntos fazermos avançar o negócio!




Sobre a Função:

Para integrar a equipa de Analytics, procuramos alguém responsável por garantir que os dados certos chegam, no formato certo, às pessoas certas — transformando dados brutos em informação fiável, bem estruturada e pronta para análise.




Transformação e preparação de dados

- Recolher, limpar e transformar dados provenientes de múltiplas fontes

- Extrair, limpar e transformar dados provenientes de múltiplas fontes (bases de dados, APIs, ficheiros, cloud), utilizando SQL como linguagem principal

- Garantir a qualidade, consistência e integridade dos dados ao longo de todo o pipeline analítico

- Documentar regras de negócio, transformações aplicadas e critérios de validação de dados

- Identificar e resolver proativamente problemas de qualidade de dados antes de chegarem ao modelo, assim como a preocupação constante da performance da solução




Modelação para o modelo semântico (Power BI)

- Construir e manter modelos semânticos robustos no Power BI, de acordo com as normas e boas práticas definidas na equipa

- Otimizar o modelo para performance — redução de cardinalidade, gestão de DirectQuery vs Import, medidas DAX, etc

- Assegurar que o modelo semântico é reutilizável, escalável e auditável por outros elementos da equipa




Análise de dados

- Questionar os dados antes de os apresentar — validar fontes, identificar inconsistências e garantir que os números fazem sentido de negócio




Colaboração com equipas de dados

- Trabalhar com Data Engineers na definição de contratos de dados e estrutura de tabelas analíticas

- Explorar e consumir dados a partir de Lakehouses e Warehouses no Microsoft Fabric




O que valorizamos:

- Experiência de 2–3 anos em funções de análise ou engenharia analítica de dados

- Experiência intermédia/avançada com SQL — joins, CTEs, agregações, subqueries

- Experiência prática com Power BI e familiaridade com o ecossistema Microsoft Fabric

- Bons conhecimentos em construção de modelos dimensionais no Power BI — relações, hierarquias, tabelas de datas

- Bons conhecimentos em medidas de complexidade média/avançada — time intelligence, CALCULATE, variáveis, iteradores

- Boa capacidade de comunicação escrita e oral, adaptando o discurso à audiência

- Orientação para a qualidade — preocupação genuína com a fiabilidade e rastreabilidade dos dados

- Curiosidade analítica — vai além da pergunta inicial e procura o contexto que dá sentido aos números

- Proatividade e pensamento crítico




O que oferecemos:

- Dia extra de férias

- Soluções de trabalho flexível

- Comparticipação das despesas de saúde extensível ao agregado familiar

- Atribuição de subsídios de apoio aos projetos de vida pessoal e familiar

- Programa de benefícios flexíveis

- Descontos na compra de viaturas representadas pelo Grupo

- Vantagens na aquisição de produtos/serviços em diversas áreas (lazer, tecnologia, bem-estar, educação…)

- Seguro de vida

- Fundo de pensões

- Formação e desenvolvimento contínuo

- Oportunidades de progressão de carreira num grupo com forte representação nacional e internacional

- Integração em equipa dinâmica com espírito jovem.




Na Salvador Caetano ajudamos as Pessoas a moverem-se.




Na Salvador Caetano queremos contribuir para um futuro inclusivo e representativo. Acreditamos que a diversidade de talento empodera as nossas equipas, cria ambientes propícios à inovação e contribui para a evolução do negócio. Aqui incentivamos a diferença e incluímo-la. Independentemente da idade, género, etnia ou religião, promovemos a igualdade de oportunidades e garante a integração numa cultura baseada na dignidade e respeito por todas as pessoas.

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
At Bloq.it, we’ve created the world’s leading smart locker solution. Solving online deliveries by enabling everyone to participate easily, reducing delivery costs and making them more sustainable.

We’re quickly expanding, and after growing at 1000% for three years in a row, we’re now the fastest-growing Smart Locker company in the world and one of the fastest growing scale-ups in Europe.

We are in search of a Data Analyst to join our innovative team as our new #bloqstar! As a Data Analyst within our Operations Strategy team, you'll help build the analytical infrastructure that powers decision-making across field operations in nine countries - you'll be a translator of business and operational requirements into analytical demands.

You'll report to the Data Analytics Manager and work hands-on with data, models, and AI-powered tools that have direct operational impact.

What You’ll Be Doing

  • Build and maintain KPI reporting infrastructure across operational markets, ensuring data accuracy and consistency;
  • Develop predictive models to support operational planning;
  • Contribute to AI-powered tools that reduce manual work at scale across our field operations teams;
  • Analyse large operational datasets to identify patterns, anomalies, and root causes of performance deviations;
  • Translate complex analytical outputs into clear, actionable insights for operational stakeholders;
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Operations, Product, Data Platform) to align on data definitions and business logic;
  • Support the migration and integration of data sources as our operational stack evolves.

What You’ll Bring To The Table

  • 2-4 years of experience in a data analyst or data scientist role;
  • Strong proficiency in Python, SQL, Spreadsheets (Excel or Google Sheets);
  • Experience building dashboards or reporting tools (Looker or similar);
  • Ability to work with messy, real-world operational data and turn it into reliable outputs;
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced, ambiguous, environment where priorities regularly shift;
  • Strong communication skills: you can explain analytical work to non-technical stakeholders;
  • Strong business acumen: you understand operational context, ask the right questions, and know that analytical output is only as good as the problem framing behind it and the narrative presenting it.

It Would Be Great If You Would Also Have

  • Hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks;
  • Familiarity with cloud data environments and DevOps practices;
  • Experience in logistics, field services, or other operationally complex industries.

Why join us?

  • The opportunity to join our Operations Strategy team and play a pivotal role in contributing to innovative solutions that redefine Bloq.it's revolution in the smart locker industry ;
  • A dynamic and fast-paced work environment with a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning ;
  • Competitive salary and flexible benefits package, tailored to your experience and skills ;
  • Eligibility for performance-based bonus, tied to your results and designed to reward your impact ;
  • Work how you work best - we offer a remote-friendly policy and flexible hours so you can stay productive and keep life balanced ;
  • Portuguese Health Insurance including mental health ;
  • Flexible Sports and Wellness membership covering multiple gyms and studios ;
  • Unlimited days off (subject to manager approval).

We value diversity and solidarity within Bloq.it, this position is open to all candidates.

If you require any special arrangements during the recruitment process, please let us know so that we can accommodate you in the best possible way.

Ready to join the revolution?

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.




Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid







What will you do as Data Analyst:

Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.




Key qualifications:

Must have:

Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.




Nice have:

Familiarity with emissions data or GHG concepts is an advantage but not required.
Comfortable in repetitive, detail-oriented validation work.




Personal skills:

Strong attention to detail and careful execution.
Strong communication and reliability under time pressure.




What ISS Offers You:

We focus on creating an environment where you can grow and perform at your best:

Permanent contract.
Private health insurance.
22 days of annual leave + 3 additional company days to recharge.
Flexible working hours and a hybrid model that supports balance and collaboration.
Continuous learning opportunities (technical and behavioural development) to support your growth.
Modern, well-equipped office in the heart of Porto.
Free parking for your convenience.
Access to ISS Tech Communities to connect, learn and share knowledge.
Free access to the company gym to support your wellbeing.
Snacks and drinks available at the office.




Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. By creating exceptional service moments and transforming workplaces into spaces where employees feel valued, engaged and productive, we enhance productivity and help our customers to attract talent and grow their businesses. This begins with our own people through training, career development, and a supportive culture – empowering them to deliver outstanding service. We know that when our people thrive, they create spaces where our customers’ employees and businesses thrive too.




ISS is a Place to Be You.

Be who you are. Become what you want. Be part of something bigger.

Become more. Become ISS.




ISS seeks to BE a place of belonging and CREATE places where every person is welcomed, embraced, and valued for exactly who they are. Places where people feel safe, respected, represented, and supported as their authentic selves.

#LI-Hybrid

Title: Banking Sector | Data Analyst
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description


Collect, clean, analyze, and interpret data to generate actionable insights that support strategic and operational decisions
Build, maintain, and monitor data pipelines, ensuring reliable extraction, processing, and loading of data
Work closely with end-users and cross-functional teams to ensure data accessibility, usability, and stability
Transform complex information into clear, engaging, and valuable deliverables for business stakeholders
Assess business changes and requirements, evaluating their impact and associated costs
Optimize code, queries, and data processes to improve efficiency and performance
Develop and maintain Airflow pipelines for data extraction, processing, and loading
Optimize Python scripts, SQL queries, and overall data workflows
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities to enhance user experience and anticipate needs
Deliver insights and visual representations that support daily decision-making
Understand business requirements and translate them into precise data solutions



Qualifications


Proven background in data analysis, with experience transforming raw data into actionable insights
Strong expertise in designing and maintaining Airflow pipelines for ETL processes
Proficiency in Python and SQL, with experience in Oracle Database environments and API development

Title: Data Analyst - Finance (m/f/d)
HTML:
Chemical Industry | Data Analyst – Finance


Your new Company


Our client is a Global Recognized Organization operating at the forefront of advanced materials and specialty solutions. With a strong international footprint and a workforce of around 1 000 professionals.


Driven by deep scientific expertise and a strong commitment to innovation, the Organization leverages advanced capabilities in material sciences, formulation technologies, and surface modification to deliver tailored solutions.


With a clear focus on innovation, operational excellence, and professional development, this is an opportunity to join a Company that combines global scale with a dynamic and forward-thinking mindset.


Your new Role


The Data Analyst – Finance transforms complex Datasets into clear, actionable insights that support decision making across the organization. Working within a matrix structure and closely aligned with Finance, this person partners with business stakeholders to understand priorities, interpret financial and operational Data, and deliver high-quality analysis, reporting, dashboards, and presentations.


This person reports to the Business Data Manager, and will collaborate closely with the Finance, Business Leaders, Technical Specialists, and Data Governance teams to support planning cycles, improve reporting capabilities, and strengthen Data-driven decision making across the business.


The Data Analyst – Finance will:

  • Partner with Finance stakeholders to understand business priorities and deliver Data-driven insights that support operational and strategic decision-making;
  • Produce accurate and timely management reporting, analyzing financial performance, identifying variances, trends, risks, and opportunities against plans and forecasts;
  • Support planning, budgeting, forecasting, CAPEX submissions, and actuals-to-plan reconciliations, ensuring Data accuracy and consistency across business systems;
  • Manage provisions, accruals, and financial Data, ensuring information integrity, compliance with Data governance standards, and the reliability of ERP and reporting system outputs;
  • Act as a subject matter expert in key Finance areas (including cost accounting, CAPEX, pricing and margins, fixed costs, working capital, and operational finance);
  • Develop, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards, reports, and visualizations to improve business visibility, performance monitoring, and decision-making;
  • Promote self-service reporting capabilities through training, user support, and best-practice guidance on Power BI solutions and planning tools;
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to strengthen analytical capabilities, optimize reporting processes, and improve Data foundations across the organization;
  • Support business projects, process improvements, and automation initiatives that drive efficiency, performance, and continuous improvement.


A typical day will include analyzing financial performance, maintaining dashboards, supporting planning and forecasting activities, collaborating with Finance and business stakeholders, validating Data quality, and delivering insights that support key business decisions.


Please consider that this role requires availability for occasional travel to other company sites.


What you will need to succeed

As a Data Analyst – Finance, you will need:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field;
  • Professional Accounting Qualification (would be a plus);
  • +3 years of experience within International Manufacturing, Industrial, or Global Business environments.
  • +3 years of experience in Financial Analysis, Finance Support, or other analytical Finance-related roles.
  • Proven experience supporting Business Stakeholders through Financial Analysis, Performance Monitoring, and Data-Driven Decision-Making;
  • Experience developing and maintaining Power BI Dashboards, Reports, and Data Visualizations for Business Performance and Management Reporting;
  • Experience working with ERP systems, large business Datasets, and Data Governance practices to ensure Data Integrity and Reporting Accuracy;
  • Experience working within Matrix Organizations and collaborating with Cross-Functional Teams would be advantageous;
  • Experience in Budgeting, Forecasting, Financial Planning, Variance Analysis, Accruals, Reconciliations, CAPEX, Cost Accounting, Pricing & Margins, Fixed Costs, Working Capital, and Operational Finance;
  • Advanced Excel skills (including Pivot Tables, Complex Data Analysis, and Financial Modelling);
  • Knowledge of Python and/or SQL (would be a plus);
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality outputs in a fast-paced international environment;
  • Excellent communication skills – Fluency in English.


What the Company can offer you


Have the opportunity to join a cross-functional team in an international company with a multicultural working environment!


Next Steps


If you are interested in this opportunity, please send us your updated CV. If you are looking for another type of professional challenge, please contact us to discuss other career opportunities, always in complete confidence.


Data Analyst - Finance (m/f/d) Hays Working for your tomorrow

Title: Data Analyst Commercial
Description:
Job Summary

The Data Analyst transforms complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that support decision making across the organisation. Working as part of a matrix team and aligned to a specific functional area (Commercial; or Supply Chain, Procurement & Manufacturing), the role partners closely with business stakeholders to understand their priorities, interpret data in context, and deliver high quality analysis, dashboards, reporting, and presentations. The Data Analyst also supports the respective stakeholder function with projects, tools, and continuous improvement initiatives that enhance performance, drive productivity and strengthen data driven decision making, with involvement in and coordination of the optimization of functional processes, tools, and systems.

Responsibilities

Job Description


Functional Business Partnering and Insight Generation
You partner with the supported function to understand business priorities, interpret analytical results, and translate data into meaningful insights that unlock value-creation opportunities, and play an active role in the development and improvement of functional processes, tools, and systems.
You produce accurate, timely monthly management information for the area supported, including analysis, interpretation, and presentation of operational or financial results. In particular, you monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) specific to the function supported and highlight emerging issues or deviations from plan.
You support functional performance cycles such as demand planning, SKU rationalisation or sales pipeline reviews depending on the analyst group.
You provide clear and actionable analysis on trends, patterns, risks, and opportunities relevant to the supported function to support operational and strategic decision-making.
You support the preparation of internal and external presentations(such as vendor or customer meetings), ensuring insights are communicated clearly and effectively.
You act as a trusted analytical advisor within a matrix structure, ensuring strong communication and alignment with both functional leaders and the Business Data Manager.
Data Cleaning, Preparation, Validation & Governance Alignment
You ensure that data pertinent to the function supported is clean and accurate for your function across various sources, such as JDE/ERP, SalesForce, New Horizon, Zycus, Ecovadis and other systems and tools.
You conduct cleaning, validation and preparation of data for operational use and analysis, ensuring accuracy and consistency in line with data governance standards and business guidelines.
You identify data quality issues and escalate them appropriately to the Business Data Manager and Data Architecture and Governance team.
You ensure all reporting uses approved definitions, metrics, and governed datasets.
Subject-Matter Expertise, Dashboards & Visualisation
You act as a subject matter expert in a designated field (e.g. SalesForce, OTIF reports, Zycus) supporting the effective use and evolution of these tools within the function.
You maintain PowerBI and other dashboards, reports, and visualisations that enable stakeholders to easily interpret key metrics and trends.
You run and distribute reports when required and not available in self-serve mode (e.g., performance reviews, monthly business updates, functional meetings), while promoting self‑service where appropriate.
You continuously improve tools and dashboard performance, usability, and clarity.
You suggest and participate in developing improvements to dashboards and reports.
Collaboration, Capability Build and Continuous Improvement
You provide super‑user type support and training to stakeholders on designed tools pertinent to the functional group supported (e.g. SalesForce, Zycus, Ecovadis), Power BI and data‑related activities.
You promote a data-driven culture across Elementis.
Consolidate feedback on new or updated reporting requirements and support the development of these tools and new Power BI dashboards or enhancements.
Collaborate with the Business Data Manager, Technical Specialist, and Data Governance teams to improve data foundations, reporting processes, and analytical capabilities.
Participate in periodic rotation across these expertise areas to broaden capability and organisational resilience.
Functional Projects, Initiatives & Processes
You support functional projects and initiatives by providing analytical input, co-ordination capabilities, and subject-matter expertise that enable better decision‑making, higher growth or more efficient operations.
You coordinate with cross‑functional teams to ensure functional project deliverables are aligned, well‑structured, and supported by accurate data.
You contribute to,and coordinate, improvements and standardisation of functional processes and procedures, automation opportunities, and enhancements to functional performance tools in line with best practice and IT guidelines.
When agreed with the function, you act as the custodian for functional policies and procedures and ensure these updated and stored in relevant repository (e.g. SharePoint, other tools)
You support internal and external audits, and any remediation actions arising from them.


Decision making

You validate business requests relating to data changes before instructing DIG to implement changes ensuring that both functional guidelines and data governance and quality guidelines are met.

You decide how to structure analysis and visualisations to meet business needs.

You make decision on projects, including timelines and milestones.

You resolve day‑to‑day analytical issues to ensure insights are accurate, timely, and aligned with functional expectations.

What will it take

Job Competencies & key personal attributes


Business Partnering & Communication: You work effectively in a matrix, engaging stakeholders across functions and presenting insights and complex concepts in a clear, compelling way. You support your stakeholders making the right decisions.
A dedicated self-starter with high enthusiasm - you need to be self-directed, self-motivated; ability to deal with imperfect information, decision and drive to successful conclusions is paramount in this role.
Drives engagement and influences effectively - it is critical that you are a strong relationship builder and can create a climate where you are able to influence stakeholders over which you have no direct lines of authority.
Analytical Problem‑Solving and “can do” attitude: You interpret complex datasets, identify trends, and translate findings into clear, actionable insights. You need strong problem-solving and issue resolution aptitude to address the daily situations that an analyst comes across.
Attention to Detail: You ensure accuracy in data updates, data preparation (e.g. pricing change requests), reporting, and other activity management, such as finance reconciliations and variance analysis.
Planning, Project Management & Prioritisation: You manage multiple requests, balancing BAU and ad‑hoc work, and deliver outputs on time.
Team player across functional and data team with uncompromising ethics and unwavering integrity - you need to be a humble, low ego individual who is strong team player, motivated by company goals rather than individual drivers, and with unquestionably integrity.


Technical Knowledge And Skills


Proficiency in Power BI and visualisation best practices.
Excellent Excel skills, including pivot tables, vlookup, advanced functions, graphs. Statistical knowledge is an advantage.
Proven track record of successful partnering with business leadership.
Advanced skills in data collections, cleansing and validation in order to minimize errors and discrepancies.
Strong analytical and quantitative skills.
Knowledge of project management tools.
Familiarity with Python or other analytical tools is an advantage.


Position Requirements

Education:


Bachelor’s degree in an analytical or business-related subject (required).


Experience:


3+years work experience including experience in an international manufacturing/industrial environment.
3+ years work experience in an analytical or support role within a function: Sales/Commercial, Supply Chain, Procurement, or Manufacturing.
Demonstrated ability to deliver high‑quality dashboards, reports, and insights.
Experience working cross‑functionally and partnering with business stakeholders.
Experience of having managed projects.


Experience of operating under matrix management is a distinctive advantage.

IT Skills: Proficient Power BI (required), Proficient or expert level in MS Excel (required); Experience of using project management tools; Python, SQL, SharePoint management (is an advantage but not a must). Language Skills: Verbal and written fluency in English.

Working arrangements: The role is full-time and based at the Porto Hub (minimum 3 days per week in the office).

Traveling required: Occasional international travel to other Elementis sites as required.

Title: Sustainability Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.

Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid

What Will You Do As Data Analyst


Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.


Key Qualifications

Must have:


Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.

Title: Data Science Analyst
Description:
Who we are




We are Metyis, a forward-thinking, global company that develops and delivers solutions around Big Data, Digital Commerce, Marketing and Design and provides Advisory services. We have offices in 15 locations with a talent pool of 1000+ employees and more than 50 nationalities, dedicated to creating long-lasting impact and growth for our business partners and clients.

Together with HUGO BOSS, our esteemed business partner, we have embarked on a joint venture and created the HUGO BOSS Digital Campus, dedicated to increasing the data analytics, eCommerce and technology capabilities of the company and boosting digital sales. The HUGO BOSS Digital Campus employees will help create a state-of-the-art data architecture infrastructure, advanced business analytics, and the development and enhancement of HUGO BOSS’ eCommerce platform and services.

This collaborative environment will provide the capabilities required for HUGO BOSS to maximise data usage and support its growth trajectory towards becoming the leading premium tech-driven fashion platform worldwide.




We are Metyis. Partners for Impact.




What We Offer




Opportunity to accelerate the pace of digitalization through advanced technology, business intelligence, and analytics.
Driving high-impact insights enhancing decision making across the entire organization.
Interaction with senior business leaders on regular basis to drive their business towards impactful change.
Become part of a fast-growing international and diverse team.




What You Will Do




Translate business problems into analytical solutions, from requirement gathering to model deployment and monitoring.
Build, deploy, and maintain machine learning models (e.g. Churn, CLV, Segmentation, Personalization) using modern MLOps frameworks (e.g. MLflow, Git).
Leverage Databricks, Azure, or similar cloud infrastructure for scalable data processing and model deployment.
Use Python, PySpark, and common ML libraries (e.g. scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, etc.) for data transformation, model development, and evaluation.
Apply statistical methods (e.g. hypothesis testing, A/B testing, confidence intervals) to validate performance and derive actionable insights.
Communicate findings and recommendations clearly through compelling storytelling and visualizations.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders (e.g. Business teams, Data Engineering, IT teams) and contribute to customer-centric strategies.
Support team development through knowledge sharing, collaboration, and participation in agile working models.




What You Will Bring




0–3 years of experience in data science or advanced analytics.
A master's degree in a quantitative field such as Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Computer Science, or Engineering.
Proven ability to manage the full lifecycle of an ML solution; business understanding, data wrangling, and modelling, to production deployment and monitoring.
Hands-on experience with MLflow, Git, and MLOps frameworks.
Solid programming skills in Python, including libraries like pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, matplotlib, seaborn, and PySpark.
Experience with Azure (preferred) or AWS, especially in the context of Databricks, data pipelines, and model hosting.
Good understanding of data warehousing and querying using SQL.
Familiarity with statistical concepts (e.g. experiment design, hypothesis testing, p-values).
Experience or strong interest in Generative AI applications, such as LLM-based solutions, prompt engineering, RAG pipelines, or AI-assisted analytics is a plus.
Exposure to Reinforcement Learning concepts is a plus, especially in areas like decision optimization, personalization, or next-best-action use cases.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, including the ability to present technical insights to non-technical audiences.
Fluency in English (written and spoken).
Experience within the retail and fashion industry (nice to have).
Experience within international environments, consultancies, or a start-up environment (nice to have).




In a changing world, diversity and inclusion are core values for team well-being and performance. At Metyis, we want to welcome and retain all talents, regardless of gender, age, origin or sexual orientation, and irrespective of whether or not they are living with a disability, as each of them has their own experience and identity.

Title: Analyst Trainee para a Unidade de Análise de Operações
Description:
Country: Portugal

COMEÇA AQUI

O Santander (www.santander.com) está a evoluir de uma marca global de grande impacto para uma organização orientada pela tecnologia, e as nossas pessoas estão no centro desta jornada.

Juntos, estamos a liderar uma transformação centrada no cliente, que valoriza o pensamento crítico, a inovação e a coragem de desafiar o que é possível.

Esta é mais do que uma mudança estratégica. É uma oportunidade para pessoas motivadas crescerem, aprenderem e fazerem uma diferença real.

A nossa missão é contribuir para que mais pessoas e empresas prosperem. Adotámos uma cultura de risco sólida, e todas as nossas pessoas, em todos os níveis, têm um papel ativo e responsável na gestão de riscos.

O TEU IMPACTO

O Santander Portugal está à procura de um Analyst Trainee para a Unidade de Análise de Operações motivado(a), com forte curiosidade intelectual e vontade de aprender, para integrar a equipa de Riscos de Crédito no Porto.

Nesta função, terás a oportunidade de trabalhar lado a lado com uma equipa experiente e multidisciplinar, desenvolvendo competências técnicas e analíticas essenciais na avaliação de risco de crédito em operações de financiamento de pequenos negócios ou empresas.

A área de riscos de Crédito é a unidade especializada na análise de operações de crédito propostas pelos nossos clientes em todos os segmentos de clientes do banco (Particulares, empresas e Grandes Empresas

Estamos a redefinir a forma como trabalhamos através da inovação, da tecnologia de ponta, da colaboração e da liberdade para explorar novas ideias.

Para ter sucesso nesta função, serás responsável por:


Analisar as propostas em fase de admissão que sejam remetidas pelo motor de decisão automático e que não sejam decididas por esse sistema para todos os produtos de retalho, para o segmento de Clientes Particulares, nomeadamente: crédito habitação, crédito ao consumo e cartões de crédito, avaliando a solvabilidade conforme as regras macroprudenciais e perfil dos clientes, garantindo o cumprimento dos critérios de avaliação de risco do Santander;
Cumprir com os níveis de serviço (SLA) estabelecidos, assegurando a resposta adequada e atempada no processo de admissão de risco de crédito particulares;
Analisar pedidos de alterações aos contratos em curso
Analisar pedidos de renegociação das condições contratuais motivadas por eventuais incapacidades de pagamento do crédito (Clientes PARI e PERSI), entre outros;
Decidir as operações analisadas dentro dos poderes de crédito delegados ou emitir parecer com apresentação e submissão das propostas a comissão de crédito;
Propor melhorias / fine tuning de regras de admissão que possam ser industrializadas e incorporadas no motor de decisão, com base na experiência da atividade gerida, que contribuam para o aumento da eficiência do sistema de admissão.


O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

Experiência Profissional


Experiência prévia em estágio (preferencialmente numa Big4) é valorizada, mas não obrigatória — o mais importante é a motivação e vontade de aprender.


Formação Académica


Formação académica concluída ou em fase de conclusão em Economia, Gestão, Finanças, Contabilidade, ou áreas similares.


Idiomas


Domínio de inglês.
Conhecimento de espanhol (valorizado).


Hard Skills


Bons conhecimentos de Microsoft Office, nomeadamente Excel avançado e Powerpoint (conhecimentos de Power BI são valorizados);


Soft Skills


Sentido de responsabilidade e compromisso com a qualidade do trabalho.
Atenção ao detalhe e foco no rigor.
Vontade genuína de aprender, crescer e assumir novos desafios.
Capacidade analítica e curiosidade intelectual.
Capacidade de gerir pressão e de manter a qualidade do trabalho em momentos de maior exigência.
Boa organização, capacidade de gerir prioridades.
Gosto por trabalhar em equipa e comunicar de forma clara.
Interesse por análise empresas, mercados financeiros e risco.


VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.


Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.


Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.
Show more

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.

  • Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
  • A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
  • Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
  • Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
  • Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.

Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.

Title: AI & Business Intelligence internship
Description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY:

ALTEN is a multinational group created more than 30 years ago, specialized in consulting services, information technology and engineering services to the main companies in the market. We achieved a turnover of more than 4.14 billion euros in 2024 and now have 57 700 employees, 88% of whom are consulting engineers.

ABOUT PORTO & POSITION:

To support our European intensive growth, the Group has decided to build a Center based in Porto with the aim to provide administrative and financial support to the European subsidiaries. In this context, we are looking for a AI & Business Intelligence internship. This role combines finance operations with data, Power BI, and AI-driven solutions, providing strong exposure to innovative projects and decision-making processes.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Support budgeting, forecasting, and monthly closing activities,
Analyze financial performance (actual vs. budget, trends),
Build and improve dashboards for management,
Audit and standardize Excel datasets for Power BI,
Develop Power BI reports and dashboards,
Design AI tools to automate analysis and detect deviations,
Generate automated financial insights and KPI analysis,
Support users, testing, and documentation.

:Bachelor's or Master’s student (Engineering, Data, BI, or similar) eligible for an IEFP internship
,Good level of english
,Strong Excel skills
,Knowledge of Power BI / Power Query
,Interest in Artificial Intelligence
,Analytical, autonomous, and business-oriented

Title: Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
As a Senior Data Analyst, you will take ownership of Körber’s marketing analytics reporting landscape, with a strong focus on Power BI and stakeholder-facing dashboards. You will be responsible for managing the Power BI workspace, maintaining and improving reporting standards, and ensuring business users have reliable and actionable insights across marketing, website, and CRM data. Working as part of the data team, you will play a central role in translating data into decisions across the marketing organization. Your focus will be on delivering high-quality reporting, answering ad hoc analytical questions, and driving continuous improvement in analytics for stakeholders. Our tech stack includes Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, and a central data lake.

Your role in our team



Own and manage the Power BI workspace for marketing, ensuring dashboards and reports are accurate, scalable, and user-friendly.
Develop, maintain, and optimize Power BI dashboards for key marketing stakeholders across campaign, website, and CRM performance.
Act as a point of contact for analytics topics within marketing, translating business questions into clear analyses and actionable insights.
Analyze marketing, web, and CRM data to identify trends, opportunities, and areas for improvement.
Collaborate closely within the data team to ensure reporting is based on reliable and well-structured data.
Identify gaps in available data and define requirements for new data sources or improvements to existing datasets.
Drive best practices for reporting design, KPI definitions, documentation, and workspace governance within Power BI.
Support ad hoc requests from stakeholders with clear analysis, visualizations, and recommendations.
Present findings to technical and non-technical audiences in a clear and business-relevant way.



Your Profile



Strong experience with Power BI, including dashboard development, data modeling, workspace management, and report optimization.
Strong SQL skills and confidence working with structured data in modern cloud-based data environments.
Solid understanding of marketing analytics, including website, campaign, and CRM data.
Ability to translate stakeholder needs into effective dashboards, KPIs, and analytical outputs.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a proactive and ownership-driven mindset.
Experience working closely with cross-functional stakeholders in a business-facing analytics role.
Excellent communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to explain complex findings in a simple way.
Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, and data lake environments is a plus.



Your Benefits

Our benefits vary depending on the country of employment. Country-specific benefits will be discussed individually during the interview process.

Your working environment

As a groupwide shared service organization, Körber Global Business Services GmbH consolidates central functions in the areas of HR, IT, and Finance. We support the companies of the Körber Group with efficient processes and standardized service offerings, thereby making a significant contribution to the success of the international Group.

This position is covered by the Collective Labour Agreement (Contrato Coletivo de Trabalho da FETESE)

Title: Digital Transformation Analyst
Description:
M/F

Location: Porto, Portugal




ABOUT US

PARFOIS is a fast-growing women’s accessories brand based in Portugal, present in over 70 countries with more than 1,000 stores worldwide.




We operate in a dynamic and evolving environment, where data, innovation, and efficiency are key to driving business performance. We are looking for analytical and driven professionals who are eager to contribute to our digital transformation journey.




THE ROLE

As Digital Transformation Analyst (Controlling), you will play a key role in improving the accessibility, quality, and structure of business data, supporting the Buying Controlling team in data-driven decision-making.




You will contribute to the development of data solutions, reporting tools, and dashboards, enabling greater autonomy and efficiency across teams.




This role is ideal for someone with a strong analytical mindset, technical expertise in data tools, and a genuine interest in transforming data into business impact.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Develop and optimize data queries, data models, and automated reports to support business needs
Improve accessibility, reliability, and quality of business information for decision-making
Design and implement data visualization tools and dashboards for business users
Enable greater autonomy of buying and business teams in accessing and analyzing performance data
Contribute to data governance initiatives, improving data structure, consistency, and processes
Support the digital transformation of the Commercial Controlling area through new tools and solutions
Reduce manual processes through automation and optimization of reporting workflows







PROFILE

Degree in Management, Data, Engineering, or a related field
Advanced knowledge of Excel and strong experience with data analysis
SQL knowledge (intermediate to advanced)
Experience with Power BI or similar data visualization tools
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to structure and interpret large volumes of data
Understanding of data models and business reporting
Detail-oriented, structured, and solution-driven mindset
Strong collaboration skills and ability to work with cross-functional teams
Proactive and pragmatic approach




Apply now!

Title: Graduate Programme 2027: Data Scientist and Analyst
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Graduate Programme

Our Graduate Programme is built for ambitious graduates who want to launch their careers in fintech. Over this 12-month programme, you’ll work on real projects, solve complex challenges, and make an impact from day one. With structured training and hands-on experience, you’ll grow your skills while exploring different areas of the business.

Top performers can be promoted during or after the programme, giving you the chance to continue your journey at Revolut.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Scientists and Analysts work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for Graduate Data Scientists and Analysts who are analytical, curious, and ready to deliver impact with data.

You’ll begin with an immersive boot camp designed to give you hands-on experience across the full data lifecycle. After that, you'll have the opportunity to join a dedicated team, like Machine Learning or Data Engineering.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let’s get in touch.

Join us from anywhere

The Graduate Programme is open to international applicants willing to relocate to one of our tech hubs in Poland, Portugal, Spain, the UK, or the UAE. We’ll support you with visa assistance, travel costs, and accommodation (conditions apply). You’ll be expected to spend around 3 days per week in the office (where role and location allow).

Key dates


Applications: open from May 2026
Recruitment process: July–December 2026
Programme start: early 2027 (January–June) or late 2027 (July–December)


Roles are filled on a rolling basis, so apply early. You can specify your preferred intake during the recruitment process.

What You'll Be Doing


Taking part in a hands-on bootcamp and working on real projects across multiple teams
Helping to build analytical tools and machine learning models (such as dashboards and ETLs) to support product launches
Improving visibility in user sign-up flows and operational funnels
Enhancing and maintaining data infrastructure (including ETLs and LookML) to keep data accessible and reliable
Collaborating with teams across Operations, Growth, and Product to refine features and uncover insights
Supporting decision-making with ad-hoc analysis (e.g., interest rate sensitivity, incentive profitability, conversion rates)
Learning from industry leaders and staying aligned with team priorities through weekly meetings
Managing tasks, tracking progress, and communicating key outcomes
Sharing updates with our global teams
Receiving regular feedback to guide your development


What You'll Need


A predicted or achieved 2:1 (or equivalent grade) in computer science, maths, physics, or a related field
To have graduated in 2025, 2026, or 2027
Impressive Python and SQL skills
A solid foundation in maths and statistics
An interest in analytics engineering, product analytics, data science, or ML engineering
Great problem-solving skills and a proactive mindset
Fluency in English and excellent communication skills
Full-time availability from early or late 2027
The ability to attend a Revolut office in your country of employment at least 3 days per week (this is a hybrid role)


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Title: Distributed Cloud | Data Analyst
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description

We are looking for a sharp and insightful Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence (BI) team. You will be the crucial link between raw data and strategic decisions, responsible for gathering business requirements, transforming complex data, and creating compelling visualizations that drive action across the organization.

Key Responsibilities


Gather and translate business needs into technical requirements for data models and reporting solutions.
Develop, optimize, and execute SQL queries to extract, transform, and load data from various sources, ensuring data accuracy and integrity.
Design and build dynamic dashboards and reports, primarily using Power BI, to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide actionable business insights.
Perform root-cause analysis on data discrepancies and collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure data quality and availability.
Present findings and strategic recommendations to stakeholders, clearly communicating complex analytical concepts to non-technical audiences.
Participate in the full lifecycle of BI projects, from requirements gathering to final deployment.



Qualifications


3+ years of proven experience as a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or in a similar role focused on data and reporting.
Strong mandatory proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience with Python for data analysis, data manipulation, automation, and scripting (e.g., using Pandas, NumPy, or similar libraries).
Expert hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI (or similar BI tools) for data modeling, visualization, and publishing reports.
Proven ability to gather, document, and manage business requirements effectively.
Excellent communication, presentation, and storytelling skills with data.
Experience with Microsoft Excel for data analysis and modeling is expected.



Valued Skills:


Experience with other components of the Microsoft Data Stack (e.g., Azure Synapse, Azure Analysis Services).
Knowledge of Data Modeling principles (e.g., Dimensional Modeling).
Professional fluency (B2+) in French is highly valued and may be a mandatory requirement for allocation on specific projects with Francophone clients.



Additional Information

The Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

Title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
Description:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:


Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
Label or classify content based on project guidelines
Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material


Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $17 per hour equivalent.

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in any discipline
Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities

Title: Data Visualization Engineer
Description:
Build. Test. Deliver. Enjoy at Pepco!

About The Role

At Pepco, we’re growing fast and building our Tech Hub in Porto to support our business across Europe.

We are looking for a Data Visualization Engineer to join the team. In this role, you will  design and sustain a robust Power BI ecosystem that enables reliable, scalable, and secure data-driven decision-making across the organization. This includes developing and maintaining a well-structured semantic data layer, governing the Power BI platform, and delivering high-quality dashboards and reports. The role balances data modeling, BI platform governance, and report development, ensuring that business users have access to trusted data, consistent metrics, and intuitive visualizations. By establishing strong foundations and standards, this position empowers teams to effectively leverage self-service BI while maintaining control, performance, and data integrity.

If you like things simple, practical and with real ownership, you’ll fit right in.

What You’ll Be Doing


Semantic Modeling & Data Foundations
Design, develop, and maintain scalable semantic models (datasets, data marts) in Power BI
Implement best practices in data modeling (star schema, reusable measures, KPI definitions)
Ensure consistency and standardization across all reporting layers
Power BI Governance & Platform Ownership
Define and manage Power BI workspace architecture (dev/test/prod separation, naming standards)
Govern access control, roles, and permissions, ensuring data security and compliance
Establish content lifecycle management (certification, promotion, versioning)
Monitor usage, adoption, and performance across the platform
Drive governance policies to enable controlled self-service BI
Dashboard & Report Development
Design and build interactive dashboards and reports aligned with business needs
Translate complex data into clear, actionable visual insights
Apply data visualization best practices (UX, storytelling, usability)
Optimize report performance and usability
Work closely with stakeholders to iterate and improve reporting solutions
Collaboration & Leadership
Partner with data engineers to ensure high-quality upstream data
Support and mentor analysts and report developers
Act as a subject matter expert for Power BI across the organization

What We’re Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Data Management, Computer Science, or a related field. Certification in Microsoft Power BI and Azure is a plus.
 3+ years of professional experience in Power BI / BI development.
Strong expertise in Power BI semantic modeling (Tabular models)
Advanced knowledge of DAX, SQL, and data modeling techniques
Proven experience with Power BI Service (workspaces, apps, permissions)
Hands-on experience building dashboards and reports in Power BI
Experience implementing PBI service governance and access control
Understanding of data lakes, data warehousing, and data modelling principles.
Familiarity with version control, CI/CD pipelines, and agile delivery methods.
Comfortable working in cross-functional product and platform teams.


What We Offer


Hybrid model: 2 days remote / 3 days onsite
Office based in Porto: 📍 https://maps.app.goo.gl/Nn7hc6uYrmPiT8Fc9
Competitive salary and annual bonus
Health insurance from the month after joining
Meal allowance
Employee discount in Pepco stores in Portugal
Learning and development opportunities
International exposure and collaboration across markets
A simple, down-to-earth culture where people support each other


About Pepco

Pepco is one of Europe’s fastest-growing retail companies, with thousands of stores and millions of customers visiting us every month. Behind that growth, there’s a team of people who make things happen. We keep things simple, we focus on results, and we support each other to get there. At Pepco, no two days are the same. And that’s exactly how we like it.

Interested? Apply now and be part of building something new in Porto!

Title: Junior DLP (Data Loss Prevention) Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) Analyst to join an international cybersecurity team focused on protecting sensitive information and strengthening data security controls across a large and complex global environment.

This role is ideal for professionals with experience in Cybersecurity Operations, Data Protection, Security Monitoring, or Information Security Analysis who are passionate about data protection and security risk management.

You will play a key role in monitoring, investigating, and improving Data Loss Prevention controls, while working closely with security teams and stakeholders across multiple international locations.




Key Responsibilities

Assess and analyze alerts generated by Data Loss Prevention (DLP) tools according to defined procedures.
Perform Level 1 triage and initial investigation of security events.
Escalate complex cases to advanced analysis when required.
Conduct Level 2 investigations to confirm and assess potential data leakage incidents.
Review and analyze security events to identify weaknesses and improvement opportunities within DLP monitoring processes.
Collaborate with managers and stakeholders regarding DLP detections, unusual activity, and rule optimization.
Recommend best practices to improve detection quality and reduce false positives.
Contribute to the testing, validation, and implementation of new DLP policies.
Coordinate policy reviews and ensure alignment with approved security practices.
Monitor entities' compliance with operational processes and service expectations.
Track and report on backlog management and remediation activities.
Produce and maintain monthly DLP dashboards and reporting metrics.
Support the onboarding and deployment of new business entities and scopes into the DLP platform.




Required Profile

Experience in Cybersecurity Operations, Information Security, SOC, DLP, or a related area.
Strong analytical and investigative mindset.
Ability to assess security events and determine appropriate actions.
Experience working with security monitoring platforms and incident analysis processes.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, international environment.
Proactive approach to identifying improvements and strengthening security controls.




Language Requirements

English: Expert level (mandatory)
French: Professional working proficiency / Mastery (mandatory)




Hybrid (2–3 days per week in the office)

Acreditamos em projetos desafiantes e motivadores, conduzidos por equipas multidisciplinares e ágeis - e estamos à procura de quem queira crescer connosco.

Neste momento, estamos a reforçar a equipa de um dos nossos clientes com o seguinte perfil:




Data Scientist / LLM Engineer - Híbrido (2 dias/semana no escritório - Porto)




Estamos à procura de um(a) LLM Engineer / Data Scientist com pelo menos 2 anos de experiência em Inteligência Artificial, Large Language Models (LLMs) e Machine Learning.




Responsabilidades

Desenhar, desenvolver e otimizar soluções baseadas em LLMs (fine-tuning, RAG, prompt engineering, agents)
Analisar dados, construir modelos preditivos e extrair insights que suportem decisões de negócio
Construir e manter pipelines de dados para treino, avaliação e monitorização de modelos
Integrar LLMs e modelos de ML em produtos e sistemas existentes, garantindo escalabilidade e performance
Colaborar com equipas de produto e engenharia para levar soluções de IA a produção
Monitorizar performance de modelos em produção (custo, latência, qualidade) e propor melhorias contínuas
Manter-se atualizado(a) sobre as últimas tendências, frameworks e ferramentas no ecossistema de IA/ML




Requisitos

2+ anos de experiência em AI, LLMs, Machine Learning e Data Science
Sólidos conhecimentos de Python e frameworks como PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, LangChain/LlamaIndex
Experiência prática com LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, modelos open-source como Llama, Mistral, etc.)
Conhecimentos sólidos de fine-tuning, RAG, embeddings, vector databases e prompt engineering
Experiência em análise de dados, estatística e construção de modelos preditivos (regressão, classificação, clustering, etc.)
Conhecimentos de SQL e ferramentas de visualização de dados (ex: Pandas, Matplotlib, Power BI/Tableau)
Experiência com deployment de modelos/aplicações em ambientes cloud (AWS, GCP ou Azure)
Conhecimentos de MLOps/LLMOps é uma mais-valia
Boa capacidade de comunicação e trabalho em equipa
Inglês fluente

Title: Business Intelligence /Data Analytics Engineer Trainee (IEFP)
Description:
Join us as a Business Intelligence /Data Analytics Engineer Trainee!

As a Business Intelligence /Data analytics Engineer Trainee you will design, build, and maintain scalable analytics solutions that transform raw data into actionable insights. You will work closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and analysts to deliver reliable data models, dashboards, and reporting solutions that support strategic and operational decision-making.

Key Accountabilities

You will primarily be responsible for:


Assist in collecting, cleaning, transforming, and validating data from multiple sources.
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, anomalies, and business insights.
Develop and evaluate machine learning models for supervised and unsupervised learning tasks.
Prepare datasets for statistical analysis and predictive modelling.
Stay current with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in data engineering, analytics, and data science.
Designing and maintaining semantic data models (star/snowflake schemas) optimized for analytics and reporting.
Building and optimizing ETL/ELT pipelines from multiple data sources, ensuring data quality and consistency.
Ensure data quality, consistency and performance across business intelligence solutions.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and reports using BI tools such as Power BI, Python and R.
Translating business requirements into robust BI solutions and flexible Power BI semantic models.
Optimizing SQL queries, data models and report performance for large-scale datasets, monitoring BI workloads in cloud and on-premises environments.
Collaborating with business users to understand KPIs, metrics and reporting needs, and partnering with Data Engineers, Data Scientists and Product teams.
Providing guidance and best practices to analysts and report developers.
Defining and enforcing data standards, naming conventions and documentation, supporting data lineage and metadata management.
Ensuring compliance with security and privacy requirements.


Knowledge, Skills And Experience Required


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Engineering or related fields.
Eligible for an IEFP Level VI Internship.
Good programming skills; knowledge of python, C#.
Machine learning model development and evaluation.
Data wrangling using libraries such as Pandas/Modin, Polars, NumPy.
Knowledge of multiple databases such as SQLServer, PostgreSQL, DuckDB.
API-based data ingestion.
Strong SQL skills and experience with data warehouses (e.g. Snowflake, Azure Synapse, Big Query, Redshift, SQL Server).
Hands-on with Power BI (semantic models, DAX, DirectQuery) and ETL/ELT tools (SSIS, ADF, Airflow, dbt, SSIS or similar).
Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric and Azure Foundry (Nice to have).
Knowledge of data modelling techniques (Kimball, dimensional modelling).
Analytical mindset with the ability to translate business needs into data models and KPIs.
Excellent communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Organized, able to manage multiple priorities and share knowledge effectively.
Fluent in English, with good verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills.
Nice to have: experience with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS), Python, DevOps/CI-CD for BI, data governance frameworks.


Apply by submitting your CV in English.

Join Euronext – Shape capital markets for future generations.

Euronext is the leading European capital market covering the entire capital markets value chain, from listing, trading, clearing, settlement and custody to solutions for issuers and investors. We operate seven national stock exchanges, handling 25% of European lit equity trading. Our products include equities, FX, ETFs, bonds, derivatives, commodities and indices. Euronext also provides clearing and settlement services through Euronext Clearing and our network of Euronext Securities CSDs. In addition, Euronext runs MTS, one of Europe’s leading electronic fixed income trading markets, and Nord Pool, the European power market. The company has a diverse domestic and international client base.
